papers.title: Achieving Gold-Medal-Level Olympiad Reasoning via Simple and Unified Scaling
papers.abstract: Abstract A systematic approach transforms post-trained reasoning models into rigorous olympiad-level solvers through reverse-perplexity curriculum, two-stage reinforcement learning, and test-time scaling, achieving gold-medal performance on mathematical and physics competitions.  AI-generated summary Recent progress in reasoning models has substantially advanced long-horizon mathematical and scientific problem solving, with several systems now reaching gold-medal-level performance on International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) and International Physics Olympiad (IPhO) problems. In this paper, we introduce a simple and unified recipe for converting a post-trained reasoning backbone into a rigorous olympiad-level solver. The recipe first uses a reverse-perplexity curriculum for SFT to instill rigorous proof-search and self-checking behaviors, then scales these behaviors through a two-stage RL pipeline that progresses from RL with verifiable rewards to more delicate proof-level RL, and finally boosts solving performance with test-time scaling. Applying this recipe, we train a 30B-A3B backbone with SFT on around 340K sub-8K-token trajectories followed by 200 RL steps. The resulting model, SU-01, supports stable reasoning on difficult problems with trajectories exceeding 100K tokens, while achieving gold-medal-level performance on mathematical and physical olympiad competitions, including IMO 2025/USAMO 2026 and IPhO 2024/2025. It also demonstrates strong generalization of scientific reasoning to domains beyond mathematics and physics.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: От рассуждений к олимпиадным золотым медалям через curriculum learning и масштабирование
ru.desc: В работе представлен систематический подход для преобразования предварительно обученных моделей рассуждения в решатели олимпиадного уровня. Метод использует обратный curriculum на основе перплексии для supervised fine-tuning, чтобы привить модели навыки строгого поиска доказательств и самопроверки. Затем применяется двухэтапный pipeline reinforcement learning, который прогрессирует от обучения с верифицируемыми наградами к более сложному обучению на уровне доказательств. Наконец, используется test-time scaling для улучшения производительности решения задач уровня международных олимпиад по математике и физике.

en.title: Transforming AI into Olympiad-Level Problem Solvers
en.desc: This paper presents a method to enhance reasoning models so they can solve complex mathematical and physics problems at a high level, similar to gold medalists in competitions. The approach involves a reverse-perplexity curriculum that helps the model learn rigorous proof-search and self-checking techniques. It then employs a two-stage reinforcement learning process to refine these skills, starting with simpler rewards and progressing to more complex proof-level tasks. The final model, named SU-01, shows impressive performance on challenging problems and can generalize its reasoning abilities to other scientific domains.

zh.title: 将推理模型转变为奥林匹克级解题者的系统方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种系统的方法，将经过训练的推理模型转变为严格的奥林匹克级解题者。该方法通过反困惑课程、两阶段强化学习和测试时缩放等技术，显著提升了模型在数学和物理竞赛中的表现。研究表明，经过这种方法训练的模型在国际数学奥林匹克和国际物理奥林匹克中达到了金牌水平的表现。最终，模型不仅在数学和物理领域表现出色，还展现了在其他科学领域的强大推理能力。

papers.title: Causal Forcing++: Scalable Few-Step Autoregressive Diffusion Distillation for Real-Time Interactive Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel causal consistency distillation method enables efficient frame-wise video generation with reduced latency and improved quality compared to existing chunk-wise approaches.  AI-generated summary Real-time interactive video generation requires low-latency, streaming, and controllable rollout. Existing autoregressive (AR) diffusion distillation methods have achieved strong results in the chunk-wise 4-step regime by distilling bidirectional base models into few-step AR students, but they remain limited by coarse response granularity and non-negligible sampling latency. In this paper, we study a more aggressive setting: frame-wise autoregression with only 1--2 sampling steps. In this regime, we identify the initialization of a few-step AR student as the key bottleneck: existing strategies are either target-misaligned, incapable of few-step generation, or too costly to scale. We propose Causal Forcing++, a principled and scalable pipeline that uses causal consistency distillation (causal CD) for few-step AR initialization. The core idea is that causal CD learns the same AR-conditional flow map as causal ODE distillation, but obtains supervision from a single online teacher ODE step between adjacent timesteps, avoiding the need to precompute and store full PF-ODE trajectories. This makes the initialization both more efficient and easier to optimize. The resulting pipeline, \ours, surpasses the SOTA 4-step chunk-wise Causal Forcing under the \textbf{frame-wise 2-step setting} by 0.1 in VBench Total, 0.3 in VBench Quality, and 0.335 in VisionReward, while reducing first-frame latency by 50\% and Stage 2 training cost by sim4times. We further extend the pipeline to action-conditioned world model generation in the spirit of Genie3. Project Page: https://github.com/thu-ml/Causal-Forcing and https://github.com/shengshu-ai/minWM .

ru.title: Причинная дистилляция для сверхбыстрой кадр-за-кадром генерации видео
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод дистилляции причинной согласованности для эффективной генерации видео кадр за кадром с минимальной задержкой. Авторы выявили, что инициализация немногошагового авторегрессивного студента является ключевым узким местом при использовании всего 1-2 шагов выборки. Метод Causal Forcing++ использует причинную дистилляцию для обучения потока, получая контроль над одним шагом учителя между соседними временными шагами, что исключает необходимость предварительно вычислять полные траектории. Результаты показывают значительное улучшение качества, снижение первокадровой задержки на 50% и уменьшение стоимости обучения в четыре раза по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: Revolutionizing Video Generation with Frame-Wise Efficiency
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called Causal Forcing++ that improves video generation by using causal consistency distillation for frame-wise autoregression. Unlike traditional chunk-wise methods, this approach allows for faster and higher-quality video generation with only 1-2 sampling steps. The authors identify that the initialization of autoregressive models is a major challenge and propose a solution that simplifies this process, making it more efficient. Their results show significant improvements in video generation metrics while also reducing latency and training costs.

zh.title: 因果一致性蒸馏：高效逐帧视频生成的新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的因果一致性蒸馏方法，能够实现高效的逐帧视频生成，具有更低的延迟和更好的质量。与现有的分块方法相比，该方法通过因果一致性蒸馏来优化少步自回归初始化，解决了响应粒度粗糙和采样延迟的问题。我们的方法在逐帧2步设置下超越了现有的4步分块方法，显著提高了生成质量并减少了训练成本。该研究为实时交互视频生成提供了新的思路，具有广泛的应用潜力。

papers.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract SDAR enhances reinforcement learning for multi-turn agent training by integrating self-distillation through a sigmoid gate that selectively strengthens positive token-level guidance while mitigating negative teacher rejections.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a central paradigm for post-training LLM agents, yet its trajectory-level reward signal provides only coarse supervision for long-horizon interaction. On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) complements RL by introducing dense token-level guidance from a teacher branch augmented with privileged context. However, transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents proves problematic: compounding multi-turn instability destabilizes supervision, while skill-conditioned privileged guidance requires asymmetric treatment for negative teacher rejections may arise from imperfect skills retrieval or utilization. We introduce SDAR (Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning), which treats OPSD as a gated auxiliary objective while keeping RL as the primary optimization backbone. SDAR maps detached token-level signals into a sigmoid gate, strengthening distillation on teacher-endorsed positive-gap tokens and softly attenuating negative teacher rejections. Across the Qwen2.5 and Qwen3 families on ALFWorld, WebShop, and Search-QA, SDAR substantially improves over GRPO (+9.4% on ALFWorld, +7.0% on Search-QA, +10.2% on WebShop-Acc), avoids the instability of naive GRPO+OPSD, and consistently outperforms hybrid RL--OPSD baselines across model scales.

ru.title: Избирательная самодистилляция для стабильного обучения с подкреплением в мультишаговых задачах
ru.desc: В этой работе предлагается SDAR — метод для обучения многошаговых агентов на основе языковых моделей, который объединяет обучение с подкреплением с самодистилляцией. Метод использует сигмоидный шлюз для выборочного усиления полезных сигналов от учительской модели на уровне отдельных токенов, одновременно ослабляя влияние её неправильных отклонений. Ключевая инновация заключается в том, чтобы рассматривать самодистилляцию как вспомогательную задачу с управляемым весом, а не как основной механизм оптимизации, что обеспечивает стабильность при обучении на сложных многошаговых задачах. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительные улучшения производительности на бенчмарках ALFWorld, WebShop и Search-QA по сравнению с базовыми методами.

en.title: Enhancing Multi-Turn Agents with Self-Distilled Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: The paper presents SDAR, a novel approach that enhances reinforcement learning (RL) for multi-turn agents by incorporating self-distillation. It uses a sigmoid gate to selectively strengthen positive feedback from a teacher model while reducing the impact of negative feedback. This method addresses challenges in transferring on-policy self-distillation to multi-turn settings, where instability can arise from compounded interactions. Experimental results show that SDAR significantly outperforms existing methods, demonstrating improved performance across various tasks and models.

zh.title: 自我蒸馏提升多轮对话代理的强化学习
zh.desc: SDAR（自我蒸馏代理强化学习）通过引入自我蒸馏技术，增强了多轮对话代理的强化学习训练。它使用一个sigmoid门控机制，选择性地加强正向标记的指导，同时减轻负向教师的拒绝影响。SDAR将密集的标记级指导与强化学习结合，解决了多轮交互中的不稳定性问题。实验结果表明，SDAR在多个任务上显著优于传统方法，提升了模型的性能和稳定性。

papers.title: MemLens: Benchmarking Multimodal Long-Term Memory in Large Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract A new benchmark evaluates memory capabilities in vision-language models through multi-session conversations, revealing limitations of both long-context and memory-augmented approaches.  AI-generated summary Memory is essential for large vision-language models (LVLMs) to handle long, multimodal interactions, with two method directions providing this capability: long-context LVLMs and memory-augmented agents. However, no existing benchmark conducts a systematic comparison of the two on questions that genuinely require multimodal evidence. To close this gap, we introduce MEMLENS, a comprehensive benchmark for memory in multimodal multi-session conversations, comprising 789 questions across five memory abilities (information extraction, multi-session reasoning, temporal reasoning, knowledge update, and answer refusal) at four standard context lengths (32K-256K tokens) under a cross-modal token-counting scheme. An image-ablation study confirms that solving MEMLENS requires visual evidence: removing evidence images drops two frontier LVLMs below 2% accuracy on the 80.4% of questions whose evidence includes images. Evaluating 27 LVLMs and 7 memory-augmented agents, we find that long-context LVLMs achieve high short-context accuracy through direct visual grounding but degrade as conversations grow, whereas memory agents are length-stable but lose visual fidelity under storage-time compression. Multi-session reasoning caps most systems below 30%, and neither approach alone solves the task. These results motivate hybrid architectures that combine long-context attention with structured multimodal retrieval. Our code is available at https://github.com/xrenaf/MEMLENS.
affiliations: NVIDIA AI Technology Center (NVAITC), NVIDIA, Santa Clara, USA

ru.title: Память в видео-языковых моделях: диагностика и преодоление ограничений
ru.desc: В работе представлен бенчмарк MEMLENS для оценки способности видео-языковых моделей запоминать информацию в многосеансовых разговорах. Авторы систематически сравнивают два подхода: модели с длинным контекстом и агенты с увеличенной памятью, тестируя их на 789 вопросах, требующих анализа мультимодальной информации. Исследование выявило, что модели с длинным контекстом хорошо работают при коротких диалогах благодаря прямому анализу изображений, но деградируют при увеличении размера контекста, тогда как агенты с памятью остаются стабильными, но теряют визуальную точность при сжатии данных. Результаты показывают, что ни один из подходов отдельно не решает задачу, и предлагается гибридная архитектура, объединяющая длинный контекст с структурированным мультимодальным поиском.

en.title: Enhancing Memory in Vision-Language Models with Hybrid Approaches
en.desc: This paper introduces MEMLENS, a new benchmark designed to evaluate the memory capabilities of vision-language models (LVLMs) in multi-session conversations. It systematically compares long-context LVLMs and memory-augmented agents across various memory tasks, revealing that neither approach fully addresses the challenges of multimodal interactions. The study shows that while long-context models perform well in short contexts, they struggle with longer conversations, whereas memory agents maintain stability but lose visual accuracy. The findings suggest the need for hybrid models that integrate both long-context attention and structured multimodal retrieval to improve performance.

zh.title: 记忆能力评估：视觉-语言模型的新挑战
zh.desc: 本文提出了一个新的基准测试，评估视觉-语言模型在多会话对话中的记忆能力，揭示了长上下文和增强记忆方法的局限性。研究表明，记忆对于大型视觉-语言模型（LVLMs）处理长时间的多模态交互至关重要。我们引入了MEMLENS基准，包含789个问题，涵盖信息提取、多会话推理、时间推理、知识更新和拒绝回答等五种记忆能力。实验结果显示，长上下文LVLMs在短上下文中表现良好，但随着对话的增长，其性能下降，而记忆增强代理在长度上保持稳定，但在存储时间压缩下失去视觉准确性。

papers.title: SANA-WM: Efficient Minute-Scale World Modeling with Hybrid Linear Diffusion Transformer
papers.abstract: Abstract SANA-WM is an efficient 2.6B-parameter world model that generates high-fidelity 720p videos with precise camera control, achieving industrial-level quality while significantly reducing computational requirements through hybrid attention, dual-camera branches, two-stage generation, and robust annotation pipelines.  AI-generated summary We introduce SANA-WM, an efficient 2.6B-parameter open-source world model natively trained for one-minute generation, synthesizing high-fidelity, 720p, minute-scale videos with precise camera control. SANA-WM achieves visual quality comparable to large-scale industrial baselines such as LingBot-World and HY-WorldPlay, while significantly improving efficiency. Four core designs drive our architecture: (1) Hybrid Linear Attention combines frame-wise Gated DeltaNet (GDN) with softmax attention for memory-efficient long-context modeling. (2) Dual-Branch Camera Control ensures precise 6-DoF trajectory adherence. (3) Two-Stage Generation Pipeline applies a long-video refiner to stage-1 outputs, improving quality and consistency across sequences. (4) Robust Annotation Pipeline extracts accurate metric-scale 6-DoF camera poses from public videos to yield high-quality, spatiotemporally consistent action labels. Driven by these designs, SANA-WMdemonstrates remarkable efficiency across data, training compute, and inference hardware: it uses only sim213K public video clips with metric-scale pose supervision, completes training in 15 days on 64 H100s, and generates each 60s clip on a single GPU; its distilled variant can be deployed on a single RTX 5090 with NVFP4 quantization to denoise a 60s 720p clip in 34s. On our one-minute world-model benchmark, SANA-WM demonstrates stronger action-following accuracy than prior open-source baselines and achieves comparable visual quality at 36times higher throughput for scalable world modeling.

ru.title: Эффективная модель мира для синтеза видео высокого качества с контролем камеры
ru.desc: SANA-WM — это эффективная модель мира с 2.6 миллиардами параметров, которая генерирует видео разрешением 720p в течение одной минуты с точным управлением камерой. Модель использует гибридное внимание, комбинируя линейное внимание с механизмом softmax, и двухэтапный конвейер генерации для улучшения качества и консистентности. Архитектура включает специальный двухветвевой контроль камеры для соблюдения 6-градусной траектории и надежный конвейер аннотирования, извлекающий точные метрические позы камер из открытых видео. Модель достигает промышленного качества при значительном снижении вычислительных требований, генерируя 60-секундный клип на одном GPU и обеспечивая 36-кратное увеличение пропускной способности по сравнению с существующими аналогами.

en.title: Efficient Video Generation with SANA-WM
en.desc: SANA-WM is a cutting-edge world model designed to generate high-quality 720p videos efficiently, utilizing only 2.6 billion parameters. It employs innovative techniques such as Hybrid Linear Attention for better memory management, Dual-Branch Camera Control for precise movement tracking, and a Two-Stage Generation Pipeline to enhance video quality. The model is trained on a limited dataset of 213,000 public video clips, yet it achieves impressive results in both action-following accuracy and visual fidelity. Overall, SANA-WM sets a new standard in video generation by balancing high performance with reduced computational demands.

zh.title: 高效生成720p视频的世界模型SANA-WM
zh.desc: SANA-WM是一个高效的世界模型，拥有2.6亿个参数，能够生成高保真度的720p视频，并实现精确的相机控制。该模型通过混合注意力机制、双相机分支、两阶段生成和强大的注释管道，显著降低了计算需求，同时达到工业级的质量。其架构的四个核心设计使得模型在数据、训练计算和推理硬件上表现出色。SANA-WM在一分钟的世界模型基准测试中，展现出比以往开源模型更强的动作跟随准确性，并以36倍的吞吐量实现可扩展的世界建模。

papers.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
papers.abstract: Abstract MemEye framework evaluates multimodal agent memory by measuring visual evidence granularity and retrieval usage complexity across 8 life-scenario tasks.  AI-generated summary Long-term agent memory is increasingly multimodal, yet existing evaluations rarely test whether agents preserve the visual evidence needed for later reasoning. In prior work, many visually grounded questions can be answered using only captions or textual traces, allowing answers to be inferred without preserving the fine-grained visual evidence. Meanwhile, harder cases that require reasoning over changing visual states are largely absent. Therefore, we introduce MemEye, a framework that evaluates memory capabilities from two dimensions: one measures the granularity of decisive visual evidence (from scene-level to pixel-level evidence), and the other measures how retrieved evidence must be used (from single evidence to evolutionary synthesis). Under this framework, we construct a new benchmark across 8 life-scenario tasks, with ablation-driven validation gates for assessing answerability, shortcut resistance, visual necessity, and reasoning structure. By evaluating 13 memory methods across 4 VLM backbones, we show that current architectures still struggle to preserve fine-grained visual details and reason about state changes over time. Our findings show that long-term multimodal memory depends on evidence routing, temporal tracking, and detail extraction.

ru.title: Зрительная память агентов: от сцены к пиксель-уровню
ru.desc: Авторы представляют MemEye — фреймворк для оценки мультимодальной памяти агентов, который измеряет детальность визуальных свидетельств и сложность их использования при поиске. Основная проблема заключается в том, что существующие оценки не проверяют, сохраняют ли агенты мелкозернистые визуальные детали, необходимые для рассуждений, позволяя моделям полагаться только на текстовые описания. Новый бенчмарк включает 8 сценариев из реальной жизни с проверками, измеряющими детальность свидетельств от уровня сцены до уровня пикселей и сложность синтеза извлечённых данных. Результаты показали, что текущие архитектуры слабо сохраняют визуальные детали и плохо отслеживают изменения состояния во времени, что требует улучшений в маршрутизации доказательств и временном отслеживании.

en.title: Enhancing AI Memory: The Power of Visual Evidence
en.desc: The MemEye framework assesses how well multimodal agents remember visual information across various life scenarios. It focuses on two key aspects: the detail level of visual evidence and the complexity of how this evidence is used in reasoning. The study reveals that many existing AI models fail to retain fine-grained visual details necessary for complex reasoning tasks. Overall, the research highlights the importance of effective evidence management and temporal awareness in enhancing long-term memory capabilities of AI agents.

zh.title: 评估多模态记忆的新框架：MemEye
zh.desc: MemEye框架通过测量视觉证据的粒度和检索使用的复杂性，评估多模态智能体的记忆能力。现有的评估方法很少测试智能体是否保留了后续推理所需的视觉证据。我们提出的MemEye框架从两个维度评估记忆能力：一个是测量决定性视觉证据的粒度，另一个是测量检索证据的使用方式。我们的研究表明，当前的架构在保留细粒度视觉细节和推理状态变化方面仍然存在困难。

papers.title: Darwin Family: MRI-Trust-Weighted Evolutionary Merging for Training-Free Scaling of Language-Model Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract The Darwin Family framework enables training-free evolutionary merging of large language models through gradient-free weight-space recombination, achieving superior reasoning performance without additional training.  AI-generated summary We present Darwin Family, a framework for training-free evolutionary merging of large language models via gradient-free weight-space recombination. We ask whether frontier-level reasoning performance can be improved without additional training, by reorganizing latent capabilities already encoded in existing checkpoints. Darwin introduces three key ideas: (i) a 14-dimensional adaptive merge genome enabling fine-grained component- and block-level recombination; (ii) MRI-Trust Fusion, which adaptively balances diagnostic layer-importance signals with evolutionary search through a learnable trust parameter; and (iii) an Architecture Mapper that enables cross-architecture breeding between heterogeneous model families. Empirically, the flagship Darwin-27B-Opus achieves 86.9% on GPQA Diamond, ranking #6 among 1,252 evaluated models, and outperforming its fully trained foundation model without any gradient-based training. Across scales from 4B to 35B parameters, Darwin models consistently improve over their parents, support recursive multi-generation evolution, and enable a training-free evolutionary merge that combines Transformer- and Mamba-based components. Together, the Darwin Family demonstrates that diagnostic-guided evolutionary merging is a practical and reproducible alternative to costly post-training pipelines for reasoning-centric language models.

ru.title: Эволюционное объединение языковых моделей без обучения
ru.desc: В статье представлена фреймворк Darwin Family для безградиентного эволюционного объединения больших языковых моделей без дополнительного обучения. Основная идея заключается в перекомбинировании весов существующих контрольных точек моделей через эволюционный поиск с использованием 14-мерного адаптивного генома. Фреймворк включает инновационный метод MRI-Trust Fusion, который балансирует сигналы важности слоёв с эволюционным поиском, и Architecture Mapper для скрещивания между разными архитектурами моделей. Эмпирические результаты показывают, что Darwin-27B-Opus достигает лучшей производительности в рассуждениях без какого-либо градиентного обучения, превосходя исходные модели.

en.title: Evolving Language Models Without Training
en.desc: The Darwin Family framework allows for the merging of large language models without the need for additional training, using a method called gradient-free weight-space recombination. It introduces a 14-dimensional adaptive merge genome for precise recombination of model components and blocks. The framework also features MRI-Trust Fusion, which balances the importance of different model layers during the evolutionary process, and an Architecture Mapper that facilitates merging models from different architectures. Empirical results show that the Darwin-27B-Opus model achieves high performance on reasoning tasks, outperforming fully trained models, and demonstrates the effectiveness of training-free evolutionary merging in enhancing model capabilities.

zh.title: 无训练进化合并，提升推理性能！
zh.desc: 达尔文家族框架通过无梯度的权重空间重组，实现了大型语言模型的无训练进化合并，从而在不增加训练的情况下提升推理性能。该框架引入了三个关键概念：14维自适应合并基因组、MRI-信任融合和架构映射器，支持不同模型架构之间的交叉繁殖。实验表明，达尔文-27B-Opus在GPQA Diamond上取得了86.9%的成绩，超越了经过完全训练的基础模型。达尔文模型在参数规模从4B到35B之间持续优于其父模型，展示了无训练进化合并的可行性和可重复性。

papers.title: Beyond Individual Intelligence: Surveying Collaboration, Failure Attribution, and Self-Evolution in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-agent systems face challenges in sustained coordination and error propagation, requiring integrated approaches that enable continuous diagnosis, reorganization, and behavioral refinement across structured collaboration stages.  AI-generated summary LLM-based autonomous agents have demonstrated strong capabilities in reasoning, planning, and tool use, yet remain limited when tasks require sustained coordination across roles, tools, and environments. Multi-agent systems address this through structured collaboration among specialized agents, but tighter coordination also amplifies a less explored risk: errors can propagate across agents and interaction rounds, producing failures that are difficult to diagnose and rarely translate into structural self-improvement. Existing surveys cover individual agent capabilities, multi-agent collaboration, or agent self-evolution separately, leaving the causal dependencies among them unexamined. This survey provides a unified review organized around four causally linked stages, which we term the LIFE progression: Lay the capability foundation, Integrate agents through collaboration, Find faults through attribution, and Evolve through autonomous self-improvement. For each stage, we provide systematic taxonomies and formally characterize the dependencies between adjacent stages, revealing how each stage both depends on and constrains the next. Beyond synthesizing existing work, we identify open challenges at stage boundaries and propose a cross-stage research agenda for closed-loop multi-agent systems capable of continuously diagnosing failures, reorganizing structures, and refining agent behaviors, extending current coordination frameworks toward more self-organizing forms of collective intelligence. By bridging these previously fragmented research threads, this survey aims to offer both a systematic reference and a conceptual roadmap toward autonomous, self-improving multi-agent intelligence.

Lenovo AI Technology Center, CTOO, Lenovo
MOE KLINNS Lab, Xian Jiaotong University
School of Computer Science and Technology, Xian Jiaotong University
School of Control Science and Engineering, Xian Jiaotong University
School of Cyber Science and Engineering, Xian Jiaotong University
School of Software Engineering, Xian Jiaotong University
Shaanxi Provincial Key Laboratory of Big Data Knowledge Engineering
Sydney AI Centre, The University of Sydney

ru.title: От координации к самоусовершенствованию: замкнутые многоагентные системы с диагностикой ошибок
ru.desc: В этой работе представлен систематический обзор многоагентных систем, организованный вокруг четырёх взаимосвязанных этапов: закладка базовых способностей агентов, интеграция агентов через совместное взаимодействие, выявление ошибок через атрибуцию и автономная самоадаптация. Авторы выявляют критическую проблему: в многоагентных системах ошибки одного агента могут распространяться через всю систему, затрудняя диагностику и препятствуя саморазвитию. Исследование устанавливает формальные причинно-следственные зависимости между этапами и показывает, как каждый этап одновременно зависит от предыдущего и ограничивает следующий. Работа предлагает концептуальный дорожный путь для создания замкнутых многоагентных систем, способных непрерывно диагностировать сбои, переорганизовывать структуры и совершенствовать поведение агентов.

en.title: Towards Self-Improving Multi-Agent Systems: The LIFE Framework
en.desc: This paper discusses the challenges faced by multi-agent systems, particularly in maintaining coordination and managing error propagation. It introduces the LIFE progression framework, which consists of four stages: laying the capability foundation, integrating agents, finding faults, and evolving through self-improvement. The authors highlight the need for a unified approach that connects these stages, as existing research often treats them in isolation. By addressing the causal dependencies among these stages, the paper aims to enhance the development of autonomous multi-agent systems that can continuously improve and adapt.

zh.title: 构建自我改进的多智能体智能
zh.desc: 多智能体系统面临持续协调和错误传播的挑战，需要综合方法来实现持续的诊断、重组和行为改进。基于AI生成的摘要和大语言模型的自主智能体在推理、规划和工具使用方面表现出色，但在需要跨角色、工具和环境的持续协调时仍然有限。本文提出了一个统一的回顾，围绕四个因果关联的阶段组织，称为LIFE进程：奠定能力基础、通过协作整合智能体、通过归因发现故障、以及通过自主自我改进进化。我们还识别了阶段边界的开放挑战，并提出了一个跨阶段的研究议程，以实现能够持续诊断故障、重组结构和改进智能体行为的闭环多智能体系统。

papers.title: STALE: Can LLM Agents Know When Their Memories Are No Longer Valid?
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models struggle to update personalized memories when new evidence emerges, requiring contextual inference and commonsense reasoning to detect implicit conflicts, as demonstrated by a comprehensive benchmark and evaluation of state-aware memory systems.  AI-generated summary Large Language Model (LLM) agents are increasingly expected to maintain coherent, long-term personalized memory, yet current benchmarks primarily measure static fact retrieval, overlooking the ability to revise stored beliefs when new evidence emerges. We identify a critical and underexplored failure mode, Implicit Conflict: a later observation invalidates an earlier memory without explicit negation, requiring contextual inference and commonsense reasoning to detect. To rigorously evaluate this capability, we introduce STALE, a benchmark of 400 expert-validated conflict scenarios (1,200 evaluation queries across three probing dimensions) spanning over 100 everyday topics with contexts up to 150K tokens. We propose a three-dimensional probing framework that tests State Resolution (detecting that a prior belief is outdated), Premise Resistance (rejecting queries that falsely presuppose a stale state), and Implicit Policy Adaptation (proactively applying updated states in downstream behavior). A systematic evaluation of frontier LLMs and specialized memory frameworks reveals a pervasive gap between retrieving updated evidence and acting on it, with even the best evaluated model achieving only 55.2% overall accuracy. Models often accept outdated assumptions embedded in a user's query, and they struggle to recognize when a change in one aspect of the user's state should invalidate related memories. To establish an initial baseline for state-aware memory, we further present CUPMem, a prototype that strengthens write-time revision through structured state consolidation and propagation-aware search, suggesting that explicit state adjudication is a promising direction for robust agentic memory.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Обновление памяти LLM: от извлечения информации к адаптивному поведению
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема обновления персонализированной памяти в больших языковых моделях при появлении новых доказательств, особенно когда требуется выявить неявные конфликты между фактами без явного отрицания. Авторы представляют бенчмарк STALE с 400 экспертно проверенными сценариями конфликтов для оценки способности моделей обнаруживать устаревшие убеждения, отклонять ложные предположения и адаптировать поведение на основе обновленной информации. Систематическая оценка передовых LLM и специализированных систем памяти показывает значительный разрыв между извлечением обновленной информации и её применением на практике, при этом лучшая модель достигает только 55,2% точности. В качестве решения предлагается прототип CUPMem, который использует структурированную консолидацию состояния и поиск с учётом распространения изменений для создания более надёжной памяти агентов.

en.title: Enhancing Memory: Bridging the Gap in Large Language Models
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges faced by large language models (LLMs) in updating personalized memories when new information is introduced. It highlights a specific issue called Implicit Conflict, where new evidence contradicts previous memories without direct negation, necessitating advanced reasoning skills. The authors introduce a benchmark called STALE, which evaluates LLMs on their ability to detect and resolve these conflicts across various contexts. They also propose a new memory framework, CUPMem, aimed at improving how models revise their memories to enhance their performance in dynamic environments.

zh.title: 提升个性化记忆的智能模型
zh.desc: 大型语言模型在更新个性化记忆时面临挑战，尤其是在新证据出现时需要进行上下文推理和常识推理以检测隐含冲突。本文提出了一个新的基准测试STAL，包含400个专家验证的冲突场景，旨在评估模型在处理过时信念时的能力。研究发现，当前的模型在检索更新证据和应用这些证据方面存在显著差距，最佳模型的整体准确率仅为55.2%。为了解决这一问题，本文还提出了CUPMem原型，通过结构化状态整合和传播感知搜索来增强写入时的修订能力。

papers.title: WildClawBench: A Benchmark for Real-World, Long-Horizon Agent Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract WildClawBench evaluates language and vision-language models on realistic long-horizon tasks using actual CLI environments with real tools instead of synthetic sandboxes.  AI-generated summary Large language and vision-language models increasingly power agents that act on a user's behalf through command-line interface (CLI) harnesses. However, most agent benchmarks still rely on synthetic sandboxes, short-horizon tasks, mock-service APIs, and final-answer checks, leaving open whether agents can complete realistic long-horizon work in the runtimes where they are deployed. This work presents WildClawBench, a native-runtime benchmark of 60 human-authored, bilingual, multimodal tasks spanning six thematic categories. Each task averages roughly 8 minutes of wall-clock time and over 20 tool calls, and runs inside a reproducible Docker container hosting an actual CLI agent harness (OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex, or Hermes Agent) with access to real tools rather than mock services. Grading is hybrid, combining deterministic rule-based checks, environment-state auditing of side effects, and an LLM/VLM judge for semantic verification. Across 19 frontier models, the best, Claude Opus 4.7, reaches only 62.2% overall under OpenClaw, while every other model stays below 60%, and switching harness alone shifts a single model by up to 18 points. These results show that long-horizon, native-runtime agent evaluation remains a far-from-resolved task for current frontier models. We release the tasks, code, and containerized tooling to support reproducible evaluation.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Оценка агентов в реальных условиях: от синтетики к аутентичности
ru.desc: WildClawBench — это бенчмарк для оценки больших языковых моделей и мультимодальных моделей зрения-языка на реальных долгосрочных задачах в настоящих окружениях CLI с реальными инструментами вместо синтетических песочниц. В отличие от существующих бенчмарков, которые используют короткие задачи и имитационные API, эта работа создала набор из 60 человеческих многоязычных мультимодальных задач, требующих в среднем более 20 вызовов инструментов и 8 минут выполнения в изолированных Docker-контейнерах. Оценка проводится гибридным методом, сочетающим детерминированные проверки, аудит побочных эффектов и верификацию семантического смысла с помощью LLM-судьи. Результаты показывают, что даже лучшая модель Claude Opus 4.7 достигает только 62.2% точности, указывая на то, что долгосрочная оценка агентов остаётся нерешённой проблемой.

en.title: Evaluating AI Agents in Real-World CLI Tasks
en.desc: WildClawBench is a benchmark designed to evaluate language and vision-language models on complex, long-horizon tasks in real command-line interface (CLI) environments. Unlike previous benchmarks that used synthetic scenarios, this framework assesses models using actual tools and tasks that require multiple steps and interactions. The evaluation process combines rule-based checks, environment audits, and semantic verification by large language models to ensure comprehensive assessment. Results indicate that current leading models struggle with these realistic tasks, highlighting the challenges in long-horizon agent evaluation.

zh.title: 真实环境下的长时间任务评估挑战
zh.desc: WildClawBench 是一个评估语言和视觉-语言模型的基准，专注于现实的长时间任务，使用真实的命令行界面（CLI）环境和工具，而不是合成的沙盒。该基准包含60个由人类编写的双语多模态任务，涵盖六个主题类别，每个任务平均需要8分钟和超过20次工具调用。评估方法结合了确定性规则检查、环境状态审计和大型语言模型（LLM）/视觉语言模型（VLM）进行语义验证。结果显示，当前的前沿模型在长时间、原生运行时的代理评估中仍面临挑战，最好的模型仅达到62.2%的整体表现。

papers.title: Warp-as-History: Generalizable Camera-Controlled Video Generation from One Training Video
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel approach called Warp-as-History enables camera-controlled video generation by transforming camera-induced warps into pseudo-history representations, achieving zero-shot capability without training or test-time optimization.  AI-generated summary Camera-controlled video generation has made substantial progress, enabling generated videos to follow prescribed viewpoint trajectories. However, existing methods usually learn camera-specific conditioning through camera encoders, control branches, or attention and positional-encoding modifications, which often require post-training on large-scale camera-annotated videos. Training-free alternatives avoid such post-training, but often shift the cost to test-time optimization or extra denoising-time guidance. We propose Warp-as-History, a simple interface that turns camera-induced warps into camera-warped pseudo-history with target-frame positional alignment and visible-token selection. Given a target camera trajectory, we construct camera-warped pseudo-history from past observations and feed it through the model's visual-history pathway. Crucially, we align its positional encoding with the target frames being denoised and remove warped-history tokens without valid source observations. Without any training, architectural modification, or test-time optimization, this interface reveals a non-trivial zero-shot capability of a frozen video generation model to follow camera trajectories. Moreover, lightweight offline LoRA finetuning on only one camera-annotated video further improves this capability and generalizes to unseen videos, improving camera adherence, visual quality, and motion dynamics without test-time optimization or target-video adaptation. Extensive experiments on diverse datasets confirm the effectiveness of our method.

ru.title: Деформация как история: управление видео через траекторию камеры без обучения
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод Warp-as-History для управления генерацией видео на основе траектории камеры без обучения модели. Подход преобразует деформации, вызванные движением камеры, в псевдо-историю с выравниванием позиционного кодирования и отбором видимых токенов. Метод демонстрирует нулевой кадровый потенциал замороженной модели видеогенерации и может быть дополнительно улучшен с помощью легковесной LoRA тонкой настройки на одном видео с аннотациями камеры. Эксперименты на разнообразных наборах данных подтверждают эффективность подхода без необходимости оптимизации во время тестирования.

en.title: Zero-Shot Video Generation with Camera Control
en.desc: The paper introduces Warp-as-History, a new method for generating videos that can be controlled by camera movements without needing any prior training or adjustments during testing. This approach transforms the warps caused by camera movements into a pseudo-history format, allowing the model to generate videos that align with specific camera trajectories. By using past observations and aligning them with target frames, the method effectively utilizes a frozen video generation model to achieve impressive results in a zero-shot manner. Additionally, light fine-tuning on a single annotated video enhances the model's performance, improving the quality and dynamics of the generated videos without requiring extensive training or optimization.

zh.title: Warp-as-History：无训练的视频生成新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新方法，称为Warp-as-History，能够通过将相机引起的扭曲转化为伪历史表示，实现相机控制的视频生成。该方法无需训练或测试时优化，展现了零-shot能力。与传统方法不同，Warp-as-History通过构建相机扭曲的伪历史，利用过去的观察数据来生成目标相机轨迹的视频。实验结果表明，该方法在多种数据集上有效，提高了视频生成的相机遵循性、视觉质量和运动动态。

papers.title: RouteProfile: Elucidating the Design Space of LLM Profiles for Routing
papers.abstract: Abstract LLM profiling design significantly impacts routing performance, with structured profiles and query-level signals demonstrating superior reliability and generalization compared to flat profiles and domain-level signals.  AI-generated summary As the large language model (LLM) ecosystem expands, individual models exhibit varying capabilities across queries, benchmarks, and domains, motivating the development of LLM routing. While prior work has largely focused on router mechanism design, LLM profiles, which capture model capabilities, remain underexplored. In this work, we ask: How does LLM profile design affect routing performance across different routers? Addressing this question helps clarify the role of profiles in routing, disentangle profile design from router design, and enable fairer comparison and more principled development of routing systems. To this end, we view LLM profiling as a structured information integration problem over heterogeneous interaction histories. We develop a general design space of LLM profiles, named RouteProfile, along four key dimensions: organizational form, representation type, aggregation depth, and learning configuration. Through systematic evaluation across three representative routers under both standard and new-LLM generalization settings, we show that: (1) structured profiles consistently outperform flat ones; (2) query-level signals are more reliable than coarse domain-level signals; and (3) generalization to newly introduced models benefits most from structured profiles under trainable configurations. Overall, our work highlights LLM profile design as an important direction for future routing research.

ru.title: Структурированные профили моделей для эффективной маршрутизации запросов
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуется дизайн профилей больших языковых моделей для системы маршрутизации запросов. Авторы предлагают общее пространство дизайна профилей RouteProfile с четырьмя ключевыми измерениями: организационная форма, тип представления, глубина агрегации и конфигурация обучения. Через систематическое оценивание на трёх маршрутизаторах показано, что структурированные профили с запросо-уровневыми сигналами значительно превосходят плоские профили с доменно-уровневыми сигналами по надёжности и обобщаемости. Результаты демонстрируют важность дизайна профилей для улучшения производительности маршрутизации и обобщения на новые модели.

en.title: Optimizing LLM Routing Through Structured Profile Design
en.desc: This paper explores how the design of large language model (LLM) profiles affects the performance of routing systems in machine learning. It introduces a framework called RouteProfile, which categorizes profiles based on their structure, representation, aggregation, and learning methods. The study finds that structured profiles provide better performance than flat profiles, and that using query-level signals yields more reliable results than domain-level signals. Ultimately, the research emphasizes the importance of LLM profile design for improving routing systems and facilitating better comparisons between different models.

zh.title: 结构化配置提升LLM路由性能
zh.desc: 本文探讨了大语言模型（LLM）配置设计对路由性能的影响。研究表明，结构化的配置和查询级信号在可靠性和泛化能力上优于平坦配置和领域级信号。我们提出了一种名为RouteProfile的LLM配置设计框架，涵盖了组织形式、表示类型、聚合深度和学习配置等四个关键维度。通过对三种代表性路由器的系统评估，结果显示结构化配置在多种设置下均表现出色，尤其是在新模型的泛化能力上。

papers.title: PREPING: Building Agent Memory without Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract Preping is a framework for pre-task memory construction that uses proposer-guided synthetic practice to improve agent performance in new environments with reduced deployment costs.  AI-generated summary Agent memory is typically constructed either offline from curated demonstrations or online from post-deployment interactions. However, regardless of how it is built, an agent faces a cold-start gap when first introduced to a new environment without any task-specific experience available. In this paper, we study pre-task memory construction: whether an agent can build procedural memory before observing any target-environment tasks, using only self-generated synthetic practice. Yet, synthetic interaction alone is insufficient, as without controlling what to practice and what to store, synthetic tasks become redundant, infeasible, and ultimately uninformative, and memory further degrades quickly due to unfiltered trajectories. To overcome this, we present Preping, a proposer-guided memory construction framework. At its core is proposer memory, a structured control state that shapes future practice. A Proposer generates synthetic tasks conditioned on this state, a Solver executes them, and a Validator determines which trajectories are eligible for memory insertion while also providing feedback to guide future proposals. Experiments on AppWorld, BFCL v3, and MCP-Universe show that Preping substantially improves over a no-memory baseline and achieves performance competitive with strong playbook-based methods built from offline or online experience, with deployment cost 2.99times lower on AppWorld and 2.23times lower on BFCL v3 than online memory construction. Further analyses reveal that the main benefit does not come from synthetic volume alone, but from proposer-side control over feasibility, redundancy, and coverage, combined with selective memory updates.

ru.title: Направляемая синтетическая практика для холодного старта агента
ru.desc: Статья представляет Preping — фреймворк для конструирования памяти агента перед выполнением задач с использованием синтетической практики, направляемой специальным модулем Proposer. Вместо того чтобы строить память на основе демонстраций или реального взаимодействия в целевой среде, агент генерирует собственные синтетические задачи и практикуется на них до встречи с реальными задачами. Ключевая инновация заключается в использовании трёхкомпонентной системы: Proposer генерирует целенаправленные синтетические задачи, Solver их выполняет, а Validator выбирает полезные траектории и предоставляет обратную связь. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют, что такой подход достигает производительности, конкурентной методам на основе реального опыта, при этом снижая стоимость развёртывания в 2-3 раза.

en.title: Preping: Smart Memory for Faster Learning in New Environments
en.desc: This paper introduces Preping, a framework designed to enhance an agent's memory construction before it encounters new tasks in unfamiliar environments. It focuses on pre-task memory construction using synthetic practice guided by a proposer, which generates relevant tasks for the agent to learn from. The framework addresses the cold-start problem by ensuring that the synthetic tasks are meaningful and informative, preventing redundancy and inefficiency in memory storage. Experimental results demonstrate that Preping significantly outperforms traditional methods, achieving lower deployment costs while maintaining competitive performance.

zh.title: 智能体记忆构建的新方法：Preping
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为Preping的框架，用于在新环境中通过合成练习来构建任务前记忆，从而提高智能体的表现。智能体通常通过离线演示或在线交互来构建记忆，但在新环境中首次引入时会面临冷启动问题。Preping通过引入提议者记忆，控制合成任务的生成和存储，避免了冗余和无效的练习。实验结果表明，Preping在多个环境中显著优于无记忆基线，并且在部署成本上大幅降低。

papers.title: EvolveMem:Self-Evolving Memory Architecture via AutoResearch for LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract EvolveMem enables adaptive memory systems for LLM agents through self-evolving retrieval mechanisms that autonomously optimize configuration parameters via diagnostic modules and iterative research cycles.  AI-generated summary Long-term memory is essential for LLM agents that operate across multiple sessions, yet existing memory systems treat retrieval infrastructure as fixed: stored content evolves while scoring functions, fusion strategies, and answer-generation policies remain frozen at deployment. We argue that truly adaptive memory requires co-evolution at two levels: the stored knowledge and the retrieval mechanism that queries it. We present EvolveMem, a self-evolving memory architecture that exposes its full retrieval configuration as a structured action space optimized by an LLM-powered diagnosis module. In each evolution round, the module reads per-question failure logs, identifies root causes, and proposes targeted configuration adjustments; a guarded meta-analyzer applies them with automatic revert-on-regression and explore-on-stagnation safeguards. This closed-loop self-evolution realizes an AutoResearch process: the system autonomously conducts iterative research cycles on its own architecture, replacing manual configuration tuning. Starting from a minimal baseline, the process converges autonomously, discovering effective retrieval strategies including entirely new configuration dimensions not present in the original action space. On LoCoMo, EvolveMem outperforms the strongest baseline by 25.7% relative and achieves a 78.0% relative improvement over the minimal baseline. On MemBench, EvolveMem exceeds the strongest baseline by 18.9% relative. Evolved configurations transfer across benchmarks with positive rather than catastrophic transfer, indicating that the self-evolution process captures universal retrieval principles rather than benchmark-specific heuristics. Code is available at https://github.com/aiming-lab/SimpleMem.

ru.title: Автоматическая эволюция системы поиска памяти без участия человека
ru.desc: EvolveMem представляет самоэволюционирующую архитектуру долгосрочной памяти для LLM агентов, которая автоматически оптимизирует параметры систем поиска информации через диагностические модули. В отличие от традиционных подходов, где механизм извлечения остается неизменным, предложенный метод одновременно эволюционирует как хранящееся знание, так и функции поиска, стратегии слияния результатов и политики генерации ответов. Система использует замкнутый цикл обратной связи: диагностический модуль анализирует логи ошибок, выявляет их причины и предлагает целенаправленные корректировки конфигурации с автоматической защитой от регрессии. Результаты показывают значительное улучшение производительности на бенчмарках LoCoMo и MemBench, при этом найденные конфигурации хорошо переносятся между задачами.

en.title: EvolveMem: Adaptive Memory for Evolving LLMs
en.desc: EvolveMem is a novel memory architecture designed for large language model (LLM) agents that enhances their ability to adaptively retrieve information over time. Unlike traditional memory systems that have fixed retrieval methods, EvolveMem allows both the stored knowledge and the retrieval mechanisms to evolve together. It utilizes a self-evolving process where a diagnostic module analyzes performance, identifies issues, and suggests adjustments to improve retrieval strategies. This approach leads to significant performance gains, demonstrating that the system can autonomously optimize its memory configuration through iterative research cycles.

zh.title: 自我进化的记忆系统，提升LLM智能
zh.desc: EvolveMem 是一种自我进化的记忆系统，旨在为大型语言模型（LLM）代理提供适应性记忆。现有的记忆系统通常将检索基础设施视为固定，而 EvolveMem 通过自我进化的检索机制，允许存储的知识和检索机制共同演化。该系统利用 LLM 驱动的诊断模块，自动优化配置参数，并通过迭代研究周期进行自我调整。实验结果表明，EvolveMem 在多个基准测试中显著超越了传统方法，展示了其强大的自我进化能力。

papers.title: Realiz3D: 3D Generation Made Photorealistic via Domain-Aware Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Realiz3D addresses the domain gap between synthetic renders and real images in 3D-consistent image generation by decoupling visual domain from control signals through residual adapters and layer-specific denoising strategies.  AI-generated summary We often aim to generate images that are both photorealistic and 3D-consistent, adhering to precise geometry, material, and viewpoint controls. Typically, this is achieved by fine-tuning an image generator, pre-trained on billions of real images, using renders of synthetic 3D assets, where annotations for control signals are available. While this approach can learn the desired controls, it often compromises the realism of the images due to domain gap between photographs and renders. We observe that this issue largely arises from the model learning an unintended association between the presence of control signals and the synthetic appearance of the images. To address this, we introduce Realiz3D, a lightweight framework for training diffusion models, that decouples controls and visual domain. The key idea is to explicitly learn visual domain, real or synthetic, separately from other control signals by introducing a co-variate that, fed into small residual adapters, shifts the domain. Then, the generator can be trained to gain controllability, without fitting to specific visual domain. In this way, the model can be guided to produce realistic images even when controls are applied. We enhance control transferability to the real domain by leveraging insights about roles of different layers and denoising steps in diffusion-based generators, informing new training and inference strategies that further mitigate the gap. We demonstrate the advantages of Realiz3D in tasks as text-to-multiview generation and texturing from 3D inputs, producing outputs that are 3D-consistent and photorealistic.

ru.title: Разделение домена от управления для реалистичной 3D-генерации
ru.desc: Realiz3D решает проблему несоответствия между синтетическими рендерами и реальными изображениями при генерации 3D-согласованных изображений. Метод разделяет визуальный домен от управляющих сигналов, используя остаточные адаптеры и стратегии поуровневого денойзинга в диффузионных моделях. Это позволяет избежать нежелательной связи между наличием управляющих сигналов и синтетическим внешним видом изображений. Фреймворк демонстрирует улучшенные результаты в задачах мультивидовой генерации и текстурирования, производя выходы, которые одновременно являются 3D-согласованными и фотореалистичными.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Realistic 3D Image Generation Made Easy
en.desc: Realiz3D is a framework designed to improve the generation of photorealistic and 3D-consistent images by addressing the gap between synthetic renders and real images. It achieves this by separating the visual domain from control signals using residual adapters and layer-specific denoising techniques. This decoupling allows the model to learn how to generate realistic images without being biased by the synthetic appearance of the training data. The framework enhances the transferability of control signals to real images, resulting in better performance in tasks like text-to-multiview generation and 3D texturing.

zh.title: 解耦控制信号与视觉领域，实现真实三维图像生成
zh.desc: Realiz3D 是一个轻量级框架，旨在解决合成渲染图像与真实图像之间的领域差距。通过引入残差适配器和层特定去噪策略，该框架将视觉领域与控制信号解耦。这样，模型可以在不依赖特定视觉领域的情况下，生成既真实又具有三维一致性的图像。我们通过对不同层和去噪步骤的作用进行深入分析，进一步提高了控制信号在真实领域的可转移性。

papers.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
papers.abstract: Abstract ATLAS presents a visual reasoning framework that combines agentic operations and latent representations using functional tokens, enabling efficient training and improved performance on complex benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Visual reasoning, often interleaved with intermediate visual states, has emerged as a promising direction in the field. A straightforward approach is to directly generate images via unified models during reasoning, but this is computationally expensive and architecturally non-trivial. Recent alternatives include agentic reasoning through code or tool calls, and latent reasoning with learnable hidden embeddings. However, agentic methods incur context-switching latency from external execution, while latent methods lack task generalization and are difficult to train with autoregressive parallelization. To combine their strengths while mitigating their limitations, we propose ATLAS, a framework in which a single discrete 'word', termed as a functional token, serves both as an agentic operation and a latent visual reasoning unit. Each functional token is associated with an internalized visual operation, yet requires no visual supervision and remains a standard token in the tokenizer vocabulary, which can be generated via next-token prediction. This design avoids verbose intermediate visual content generation, while preserving compatibility with the vanilla scalable SFT and RL training, without architectural or methodological modifications. To further address the sparsity of functional tokens during RL, we introduce Latent-Anchored GRPO (LA-GRPO), which stabilizes the training by anchoring functional tokens with a statically weighted auxiliary objective, providing stronger gradient updates. Extensive experiments and analyses demonstrate that ATLAS achieves superior performance on challenging benchmarks while maintaining clear interpretability. We hope ATLAS offers a new paradigm inspiring future visual reasoning research.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Визуальное рассуждение через функциональные токены
ru.desc: ATLAS представляет фреймворк для визуального рассуждения, который объединяет агентивные операции и скрытые представления через функциональные токены. Каждый функциональный токен действует одновременно как операция агента и единица латентного визуального рассуждения, избегая необходимости генерировать промежуточные изображения. Авторы предлагают модификацию алгоритма обучения с подкреплением под названием Latent-Anchored GRPO для стабилизации обучения функциональных токенов. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что ATLAS достигает выдающейся производительности на сложных бенчмарках при сохранении интерпретируемости модели.

en.title: ATLAS: Bridging Agentic and Latent Visual Reasoning
en.desc: ATLAS is a visual reasoning framework that integrates agentic operations and latent representations through the use of functional tokens. This approach allows for efficient training and enhances performance on complex tasks by combining the strengths of agentic and latent reasoning while minimizing their weaknesses. Functional tokens act as both operational commands and visual reasoning units, eliminating the need for extensive visual supervision and reducing computational costs. The introduction of Latent-Anchored GRPO further stabilizes training by providing stronger gradient updates, leading to improved results on challenging benchmarks.

zh.title: ATLAS：视觉推理的新范式
zh.desc: ATLAS是一个视觉推理框架，它结合了代理操作和潜在表示，使用功能性标记来提高训练效率和复杂基准的性能。该框架通过将单个离散的“词”作为代理操作和潜在视觉推理单元，避免了生成冗长的中间视觉内容。ATLAS设计不需要视觉监督，并且与标准的标记器词汇兼容，能够通过下一个标记预测生成。通过引入潜在锚定的GRPO（LA-GRPO），ATLAS在训练过程中稳定了功能性标记的稀疏性，提供了更强的梯度更新。

papers.title: Learning to Communicate Locally for Large-Scale Multi-Agent Pathfinding
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-agent pathfinding solver enhanced with learnable communication module improves coordination and performance while maintaining scalability.  AI-generated summary Multi-agent pathfinding (MAPF) is a widely used abstraction for multi-robot trajectory planning problems, where multiple homogeneous agents move simultaneously within a shared environment. Although solving MAPF optimally is NP-hard, scalable and efficient solvers are critical for real-world applications such as logistics and search-and-rescue. To this end, the research community has proposed various decentralized suboptimal MAPF solvers that leverage machine learning. Such methods frame MAPF (from a single agent perspective) as a Dec-POMDP where at each time step an agent has to decide an action based on the local observation and typically solve the problem via reinforcement learning or imitation learning. We follow the same approach but additionally introduce a learnable communication module tailored to enhance cooperation between agents via efficient feature sharing. We present the Local Communication for Multi-agent Pathfinding (LC-MAPF), a generalizable pre-trained model that applies multi-round communication between neighboring agents to exchange information and improve their coordination. Our experiments show that the introduced method outperforms the existing learning-based MAPF solvers, including IL and RL-based approaches, across diverse metrics in a diverse range of (unseen) test scenarios. Remarkably, the introduced communication mechanism does not compromise LC-MAPF's scalability, a common bottleneck for communication-based MAPF solvers.

ru.title: Обучаемая коммуникация для масштабируемого многоагентного планирования траекторий
ru.desc: В работе предложен решатель задачи многоагентного поиска пути, улучшенный модулем обучаемой коммуникации для повышения координации между агентами. Метод LC-MAPF использует многораундовый обмен информацией между соседними агентами через локальную коммуникацию, что позволяет эффективнее решать задачу в рамках Dec-POMDP. Авторы применяют подход обучения с подкреплением и имитационного обучения, дополненный механизмом передачи признаков между агентами. Экспериментальные результаты показывают превосходство LC-MAPF над существующими методами на основе машинного обучения при сохранении масштабируемости алгоритма.

en.title: Enhancing Multi-Agent Coordination with Learnable Communication
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to multi-agent pathfinding (MAPF) by introducing a learnable communication module that enhances agent coordination. The method, called Local Communication for Multi-agent Pathfinding (LC-MAPF), allows agents to share information efficiently, improving their decision-making process. By framing MAPF as a decentralized partially observable Markov decision process (Dec-POMDP), the model utilizes reinforcement learning and imitation learning techniques. The results demonstrate that LC-MAPF outperforms existing learning-based MAPF solvers while maintaining scalability, making it suitable for real-world applications.

zh.title: 增强智能体合作的路径规划新方法
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种增强的多智能体路径规划求解器，结合了可学习的通信模块，以提高智能体之间的协调性和性能，同时保持可扩展性。多智能体路径规划（MAPF）是多机器人轨迹规划问题的常用抽象，解决此问题的高效求解器对于实际应用至关重要。我们的方法通过引入局部通信机制，使得相邻智能体能够高效地共享特征，从而改善合作效果。实验结果表明，所提出的LC-MAPF模型在多种测试场景中优于现有的学习基础MAPF求解器，且不影响其可扩展性。

papers.title: FrontierSmith: Synthesizing Open-Ended Coding Problems at Scale
papers.abstract: Abstract FrontierSmith automates the creation of open-ended coding problems from closed-ended tasks, improving LLM coding performance on benchmarks through diverse problem variants and enhanced agent interactions.  AI-generated summary Many real-world coding challenges are open-ended and admit no known optimal solution. Yet, recent progress in LLM coding has focused on well-defined tasks such as feature implementation, bug fixing, and competitive programming. Open-ended coding remains a weak spot for LLMs, largely because open-ended training problems are scarce and expensive to construct. Our goal is to synthesize open-ended coding problems at scale to train stronger LLM coders. We introduce FrontierSmith, an automated system for iteratively evolving open-ended problems from existing closed-ended coding tasks. Starting from competitive programming problems, FrontierSmith generates candidate open-ended variants by changing the problems'goals, restricting outputs, and generalizing inputs. It then uses a quantitative idea divergence metric to select problems that elicit genuinely diverse approaches from different solvers. Agents then generate test cases and verifiers for the surviving candidates. On two open-ended coding benchmarks, training on our synthesized data yields substantial gains over the base models: Qwen3.5-9B improves by +8.82 score on FrontierCS and +306.36 (Elo-rating-based performance) on ALE-bench; Qwen3.5-27B improves by +12.12 and +309.12, respectively. The synthesized problems also make agents take more turns and use more tokens, similar to human-curated ones, suggesting that closed-ended seeds can be a practical starting point for long-horizon coding data.

ru.title: Синтезируем открытые задачи программирования для тренировки сильнейших LLM кодеров
ru.desc: FrontierSmith — это система, которая автоматически преобразует закрытые задачи программирования в открытые проблемы для обучения LLM. Система использует метрику divergence для выбора задач, которые требуют разнообразных подходов к решению. Сгенерированные варианты проблем дополняются тестами и верификаторами, созданными агентами. На бенчмарках показано значительное улучшение производительности кодирующих моделей благодаря обучению на синтезированных данных.

en.title: Automating Open-Ended Coding Challenges for Better LLM Performance
en.desc: FrontierSmith is a system designed to automatically create open-ended coding problems from existing closed-ended tasks, which helps improve the performance of large language models (LLMs) in coding. Open-ended coding challenges are important because they reflect real-world scenarios where there is no single correct solution, but they are difficult to generate. By transforming competitive programming problems into diverse open-ended variants, FrontierSmith allows LLMs to train on a wider range of coding tasks. The results show that training on these synthesized problems significantly enhances the performance of LLMs on open-ended coding benchmarks, indicating that this approach can effectively bridge the gap in LLM capabilities.

zh.title: 自动生成开放式编码问题，提升LLM表现
zh.desc: FrontierSmith 是一个自动化系统，旨在从封闭式编码任务中生成开放式编码问题，以提高大型语言模型（LLM）在基准测试中的编码表现。通过改变问题目标、限制输出和概括输入，FrontierSmith 能够生成多样化的开放式问题变体。该系统使用定量的思想差异度量来选择能够引发不同解法的多样化问题。实验结果表明，使用合成的数据进行训练，模型在开放式编码基准测试中取得了显著的性能提升。

papers.title: Long Context Pre-Training with Lighthouse Attention
papers.abstract: Abstract Lighthouse Attention enables efficient training of causal transformers at long sequences by using hierarchical selection-based attention that reduces computational complexity while maintaining model performance.  AI-generated summary Training causal transformers at extreme sequence lengths is bottlenecked by the quadratic time and memory of scaled dot-product attention (SDPA). In this work, we propose Lighthouse Attention, a training-only symmetrical selection-based hierarchical attention algorithm that wraps around ordinary SDPA and can be easily removed towards the end of the training. Our hierarchical selection is also gradient-free, which exempts us from dealing with a complicated and potentially inefficient backward pass kernel. Our contribution is three-fold: (i) A subquadratic hierarchical pre- and post-processing step that does adaptive compression and decompression of the sequence. (ii) A symmetrical compression strategy that pools queries, keys and values at the same time, while preserving left-to-right causality, which greatly improves parallelism. (iii) A two stage training approach which we pre-train for the majority of the time with Lighthouse Attention and recover a full attention model at the end with a short training. We run preliminary small scale LLM pre-training experiments that show the effectiveness of our method compared to full attention training with all other settings matched, where we achieve a faster total training time and lower final loss after the recovery phase. Full code is available at: https://github.com/ighoshsubho/lighthouse-attention

ru.title: Иерархическое внимание для эффективного обучения трансформеров на длинных последовательностях
ru.desc: Статья предлагает Lighthouse Attention — новый метод обучения причинных трансформеров на длинных последовательностях, который решает проблему квадратичной сложности стандартного масштабируемого скалярного произведения внимания. Метод использует иерархический отбор с адаптивным сжатием и распаковкой последовательностей, сохраняя при этом причинность (левопричинное направление). Алгоритм работает только во время обучения и может быть удален перед финальной стадией, что позволяет восстановить полную модель внимания. Экспериментальные результаты на предварительном обучении LLM показывают, что метод достигает более быстрого обучения и меньшей финальной ошибки по сравнению с полным вниманием.

en.title: Efficient Long Sequence Training with Lighthouse Attention
en.desc: Lighthouse Attention is a new method designed to improve the training of causal transformers on long sequences by reducing the computational load associated with traditional attention mechanisms. It introduces a hierarchical selection-based attention approach that allows for efficient processing without sacrificing model performance. This method includes a unique compression strategy that maintains the necessary causality while enhancing parallel processing capabilities. Additionally, it employs a two-stage training process that initially uses Lighthouse Attention and transitions to full attention for final adjustments, resulting in faster training times and lower loss rates.

zh.title: 高效长序列训练的灯塔注意力
zh.desc: Lighthouse Attention是一种高效训练因果变换器的方法，特别适用于长序列。它通过分层选择的注意力机制，降低了计算复杂度，同时保持了模型性能。该方法采用了子平方的分层预处理和后处理步骤，实现了序列的自适应压缩和解压缩。实验结果表明，与传统的全注意力训练相比，Lighthouse Attention在训练时间和最终损失上都有显著改善。

papers.title: DiffusionOPD: A Unified Perspective of On-Policy Distillation in Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: Abstract DiffusionOPD enables efficient multi-task training for diffusion models through online policy distillation, outperforming existing reinforcement learning approaches in both training efficiency and final performance.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning has emerged as a powerful tool for improving diffusion-based text-to-image models, but existing methods are largely limited to single-task optimization. Extending RL to multiple tasks is challenging: joint optimization suffers from cross-task interference and imbalance, while cascade RL is cumbersome and prone to catastrophic forgetting. We propose DiffusionOPD, a new multi-task training paradigm for diffusion models based on Online Policy Distillation (OPD). DiffusionOPD first trains task-specific teachers independently, then distills their capabilities into a unified student along the student own rollout trajectories. This decouples single-task exploration from multi-task integration and avoids the optimization burden of solving all tasks jointly from scratch. Theoretically, we lift the OPD framework from discrete tokens to continuous-state Markov processes, deriving a closed-form per-step KL objective that unifies both stochastic SDE and deterministic ODE refinement via mean-matching. We formally and empirically demonstrate that this analytic gradient provides lower variance and better generality compared to conventional PPO-style policy gradients. Extensive experiments show that DiffusionOPD consistently surpasses both multi-reward RL and cascade RL baselines in training efficiency and final performance, while achieving state-of-the-art results on all evaluated benchmarks.

ru.title: Многозадачная дистилляция политики для эффективного обучения диффузионных моделей
ru.desc: DiffusionOPD представляет новую парадигму многозадачного обучения для диффузионных моделей, использующую онлайн-дистилляцию политики. Метод сначала обучает отдельные учительские модели для каждой задачи, затем дистиллирует их знания в единую студент-модель по её собственным траекториям. Авторы теоретически расширяют фреймворк OPD на непрерывные марковские процессы и выводят аналитическую KL-целевую функцию, которая объединяет стохастическое и детерминированное уточнение. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что DiffusionOPD превосходит методы мульти-награда и каскадное обучение с подкреплением по эффективности и качеству.

en.title: Efficient Multi-Task Learning with DiffusionOPD
en.desc: DiffusionOPD introduces a novel approach for training diffusion models using Online Policy Distillation, allowing for efficient multi-task learning. This method addresses the limitations of traditional reinforcement learning, which often struggles with single-task optimization and suffers from issues like cross-task interference. By training task-specific teachers independently and then distilling their knowledge into a unified student model, DiffusionOPD effectively separates the exploration of individual tasks from their integration. The framework also enhances performance by providing a more stable and generalizable training process, leading to superior results compared to existing methods.

zh.title: DiffusionOPD：高效的多任务扩散模型训练
zh.desc: DiffusionOPD是一种新颖的多任务训练方法，旨在提高扩散模型的训练效率。通过在线策略蒸馏（OPD），该方法首先独立训练特定任务的教师模型，然后将其能力蒸馏到一个统一的学生模型中。这种方法有效地避免了多任务优化中的干扰和不平衡问题，同时减少了从头开始解决所有任务的优化负担。实验结果表明，DiffusionOPD在训练效率和最终性能上均优于现有的多奖励强化学习和级联强化学习方法。

papers.title: IntentVLA: Short-Horizon Intent Modeling for Aliased Robot Manipulation
papers.abstract: Abstract IntentVLA is a history-conditioned visual-language action framework that improves robot imitation learning stability by encoding short-horizon intents from visual observations, addressing challenges from partial observability and ambiguous observations.  AI-generated summary Robot imitation data are often multimodal: similar visual-language observations may be followed by different action chunks because human demonstrators act with different short-horizon intents, task phases, or recent context. Existing frame-conditioned VLA policies infer each chunk from the current observation and instruction alone, so under partial observability they may resample different intents across adjacent replanning steps, leading to inter-chunk conflict and unstable execution. We introduce IntentVLA, a history-conditioned VLA framework that encodes recent visual observations into a compact short-horizon intent representation and uses it to condition chunk generation. We further introduce AliasBench, a 12-task ambiguity-aware benchmark on RoboTwin2 with matched training data and evaluation environments that isolate short-horizon observation aliasing. Across AliasBench, SimplerEnv, LIBERO, and RoboCasa, IntentVLA improves rollout stability and outperforms strong VLA baselines

ru.title: История наблюдений как ключ к стабильному управлению роботом
ru.desc: IntentVLA — это framework для обучения роботов на основе имитации, который улучшает стабильность выполнения за счёт кодирования краткосрочных намерений из визуальных наблюдений. Основная проблема заключается в мультимодальности данных обучения: одинаковые визуально-языковые наблюдения могут следоваться разными действиями, так как демонстраторы имеют разные цели и контекст. Существующие VLA модели, обусловленные текущим кадром, игнорируют историю и могут случайно переключаться между разными намерениями при переплань, что приводит к нестабильности. Предложенный подход использует историю визуальных наблюдений для создания компактного представления короткосрочного намерения, которое затем направляет генерацию действий и обеспечивает более стабильное выполнение задач.

en.title: Enhancing Robot Imitation Learning with IntentVLA
en.desc: IntentVLA is a framework designed to enhance the stability of robot imitation learning by utilizing history-conditioned visual-language actions. It addresses the issues of partial observability and ambiguous observations by encoding short-horizon intents derived from recent visual inputs. Unlike traditional methods that rely solely on current observations, IntentVLA incorporates a history of visual data to generate more consistent action sequences. The framework is evaluated using AliasBench, a benchmark that tests its performance across various tasks, demonstrating improved stability and effectiveness compared to existing models.

zh.title: 提升机器人模仿学习稳定性的意图框架
zh.desc: IntentVLA是一种历史条件的视觉语言动作框架，通过编码来自视觉观察的短期意图，提高了机器人模仿学习的稳定性。该框架解决了部分可观察性和模糊观察带来的挑战，能够更好地处理多模态的机器人模仿数据。与现有的基于帧的VLA策略不同，IntentVLA利用最近的视觉观察生成紧凑的短期意图表示，从而减少了不同动作块之间的冲突。通过在AliasBench等基准测试中的表现，IntentVLA展示了其在执行稳定性和性能上的显著提升。

papers.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
papers.abstract: Abstract VGGT-Edit enables text-conditioned 3D scene editing through depth-synchronized text injection and direct geometric displacement prediction, achieving superior quality and efficiency over 2D-lifting approaches.  AI-generated summary High-quality 3D scene reconstruction has recently advanced toward generalizable feed-forward architectures, enabling the generation of complex environments in a single forward pass. However, despite their strong performance in static scene perception, these models remain limited in responding to dynamic human instructions, which restricts their use in interactive applications. Existing editing methods typically rely on a 2D-lifting strategy, where individual views are edited independently and then lifted back into 3D space. This indirect pipeline often leads to blurry textures and inconsistent geometry, as 2D editors lack the spatial awareness required to preserve structure across viewpoints. To address these limitations, we propose VGGT-Edit, a feed-forward framework for text-conditioned native 3D scene editing. VGGT-Edit introduces depth-synchronized text injection to align semantic guidance with the backbone's spatial poses, ensuring stable instruction grounding. This semantic signal is then processed by a residual transformation head, which directly predicts 3D geometric displacements to deform the scene while preserving background stability. To ensure high-fidelity results, we supervise the framework with a multi-term objective function that enforces geometric accuracy and cross-view consistency. We also construct the DeltaScene Dataset, a large-scale dataset generated through an automated pipeline with 3D agreement filtering to ensure ground-truth quality. Experiments show that VGGT-Edit substantially outperforms 2D-lifting baselines, producing sharper object details, stronger multi-view consistency, and near-instant inference speed.
affiliations: Beijing Key Lab of Data Intel. & Security (PKU)
The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Прямое редактирование 3D-сцен через текстовые команды с сохранением геометрии
ru.desc: VGGT-Edit — это нейросетевая архитектура для редактирования 3D-сцен на основе текстовых инструкций, работающая напрямую в трёхмерном пространстве без промежуточного преобразования через 2D-изображения. Ключевое нововведение заключается в синхронизации текстовых указаний с информацией о глубине и прямом предсказании геометрических смещений для деформации сцены. Авторы предложили многокомпонентную функцию потерь, обеспечивающую геометрическую точность и согласованность между различными видами сцены. Метод превосходит существующие подходы 2D-lifting в качестве визуализации, консистентности и скорости инференса благодаря использованию специально созданного датасета DeltaScene с автоматической фильтрацией.

en.title: Revolutionizing 3D Scene Editing with Text Guidance
en.desc: VGGT-Edit is a novel framework for editing 3D scenes based on text instructions, improving upon traditional 2D-lifting methods. It utilizes depth-synchronized text injection to ensure that the semantic guidance aligns with the spatial structure of the scene. By directly predicting geometric displacements, VGGT-Edit maintains the stability of the background while allowing for dynamic modifications.
