The framework is trained with a multi-term objective to enhance geometric accuracy and consistency across different views, resulting in high-quality 3D scene reconstructions.

zh.title: VGGT-Edit：高效的文本驱动3D场景编辑
zh.desc: VGGT-Edit 是一种基于文本的3D场景编辑框架，通过深度同步的文本注入和直接的几何位移预测，实现了高效且高质量的3D场景编辑。与传统的2D提升方法相比，VGGT-Edit 能够更好地响应动态人类指令，适用于交互式应用。该方法通过引入语义信号和残差变换头，直接预测3D几何位移，从而保持场景的稳定性和一致性。实验结果表明，VGGT-Edit 在细节清晰度和多视图一致性方面显著优于2D提升基线，且推理速度接近实时。

papers.title: ViMU: Benchmarking Video Metaphorical Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract Video understanding models lack capability to interpret implicit meanings and social contexts beyond literal visual comprehension, necessitating new benchmarking approaches.  AI-generated summary Any new medium, once it emerges, is used for more than the transmission of overt content alone. The information it carries typically operates on two levels: one is the content directly presented, while the other is the subtext beneath it-the implicit ideas and intentions the creator seeks to convey through the medium. Likewise, since video technologies became widely adopted, video has served not only as a powerful tool for recording and communicating visual information, but also as a vehicle for emotions, attitudes, and social meanings that are often difficult to articulate explicitly. Thus, the true meaning of many videos does not reside solely in what is shown on screen; it is often embedded in context, style of expression, and the viewer's social experience. Some forms of such video subtext are humorous, while others carry irony, mockery, or criticism. These implicit meanings can also be interpreted very differently across cultural backgrounds and social groups. However, most existing video understanding models still focus primarily on literal visual comprehension, such as recognizing objects, actions, or temporal relations, and lack a systematic ability to understand the metaphorical, ironic, and social meanings embedded in videos. To bridge this gap, we introduce ViMU, the first benchmark designed to systematically evaluate the subtext understanding capabilities of frontier models in videos. ViMU assesses whether video understanding models can go beyond literal perception to infer implicit meaning while grounding their interpretations in multimodal evidence and answering both open-ended and multiple-choice questions. Importantly, all questions are designed to be hint-free, ensuring that no key evidence is disclosed to models before answering.

ru.title: От буквального видения к пониманию подтекста: оценка скрытых смыслов в видеоконтенте
ru.desc: В статье представляется новый бенчмарк ViMU для оценки способности видеомоделей понимать имплицитные значения, иронию и социальный контекст в видео, а не только буквальное визуальное содержание. Авторы подчеркивают, что существующие модели обработки видео сосредоточены на распознавании объектов и действий, но не могут интерпретировать метафорические и культурные смыслы. ViMU включает задачи с открытыми ответами и множественным выбором, которые требуют мультимодального анализа без подсказок. Этот бенчмарк позволяет систематически оценивать, могут ли frontier модели выходить за пределы буквального восприятия для понимания подтекста видео.

en.title: Unlocking Implicit Meanings in Video Understanding
en.desc: This paper addresses the limitations of current video understanding models, which primarily focus on literal visual comprehension. It highlights the importance of interpreting implicit meanings and social contexts that go beyond what is directly shown in videos. To tackle this issue, the authors introduce ViMU, a new benchmark that evaluates models on their ability to understand subtext in videos. ViMU requires models to infer implicit meanings using multimodal evidence without any hints, thus promoting a deeper understanding of video content.

zh.title: 超越字面理解，探索视频隐含意义
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了视频理解模型在解读隐含意义和社会背景方面的不足，强调了需要新的基准测试方法。视频不仅传递显性内容，还承载着隐含的思想和意图，这些通常难以明确表达。现有的视频理解模型主要关注字面视觉理解，如识别物体和动作，缺乏对隐喻、讽刺和社会意义的系统理解。为此，本文提出了ViMU基准，旨在评估前沿模型在视频中理解隐含意义的能力。

papers.title: Orchard: An Open-Source Agentic Modeling Framework
papers.abstract: Abstract Orchard is an open-source framework for scalable agentic modeling that enables training diverse autonomous agents through specialized recipes for coding, GUI navigation, and personal assistance tasks.  AI-generated summary Agentic modeling aims to transform LLMs into autonomous agents capable of solving complex tasks through planning, reasoning, tool use, and multi-turn interaction with environments. Despite major investment, open research remains constrained by infrastructure and training gaps. Many high-performing systems rely on proprietary codebases, models, or services, while most open-source frameworks focus on orchestration and evaluation rather than scalable agent training. We present Orchard, an open-source framework for scalable agentic modeling. At its core is Orchard Env, a lightweight environment service providing reusable primitives for sandbox lifecycle management across task domains, agent harnesses, and pipeline stages. On top of Orchard Env, we build three agentic modeling recipes. Orchard-SWE targets coding agents. We distill 107K trajectories from MiniMax-M2.5 and Qwen3.5-397B, introduce credit-assignment SFT to learn from productive segments of unresolved trajectories, and apply Balanced Adaptive Rollout for RL. Starting from Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking, Orchard-SWE achieves 64.3% on SWE-bench Verified after SFT and 67.5% after SFT+RL, setting a new state of the art among open-source models of comparable size. Orchard-GUI trains a 4B vision-language computer-use agent using only 0.4K distilled trajectories and 2.2K open-ended tasks. It achieves 74.1%, 67.0%, and 64.0% success rates on WebVoyager, Online-Mind2Web, and DeepShop, respectively, making it the strongest open-source model while remaining competitive with proprietary systems. Orchard-Claw targets personal assistant agents. Trained with only 0.2K synthetic tasks, it achieves 59.6% pass@3 on Claw-Eval and 73.9% when paired with a stronger ZeroClaw harness. Collectively, these results show that a lightweight, open, harness-agnostic environment layer enables reusable agentic data, training recipes, and evaluations across domains.

ru.title: Открытая фреймворк для обучения автономных агентов в разных доменах
ru.desc: Orchard — это открытая фреймворк для масштабируемого обучения автономных агентов на основе больших языковых моделей. Фреймворк предоставляет легкий сервис окружения для управления песочницами и содержит три специализированные рецепты: Orchard-SWE для агентов написания кода, Orchard-GUI для навигации по интерфейсам и Orchard-Claw для персональных ассистентов. Авторы применяют дистилляцию данных, обучение с подкреплением и методы кредитного назначения для эффективной подготовки компактных моделей. Полученные модели достигают лучших результатов среди открытых решений на бенчмарках SWE-bench, WebVoyager и других задачах.

en.title: Empowering Autonomous Agents with Orchard: Open-Source Scalability for Diverse Tasks
en.desc: The paper introduces Orchard, an open-source framework designed for scalable agentic modeling, which allows the training of various autonomous agents for tasks like coding, GUI navigation, and personal assistance. It emphasizes the importance of agentic modeling, which transforms large language models (LLMs) into autonomous agents capable of complex problem-solving through planning and interaction. Orchard provides a lightweight environment service, Orchard Env, that facilitates sandbox management and supports diverse training recipes for different agent types. The framework demonstrates significant performance improvements in open-source models, setting new benchmarks in coding, GUI tasks, and personal assistance, while remaining competitive with proprietary systems.

zh.title: 开源自主代理建模的未来
zh.desc: Orchard是一个开源框架，旨在实现可扩展的自主代理建模，支持通过特定的配方训练多样化的自主代理。该框架的核心是Orchard Env，一个轻量级环境服务，提供可重用的原语，用于管理不同任务领域的生命周期。Orchard构建了三种代理建模配方，分别针对编码、图形用户界面导航和个人助理任务，展示了在开源模型中达到新的性能标准。通过这些创新，Orchard为自主代理的训练和评估提供了一个灵活且开放的环境。

papers.title: Forcing-KV: Hybrid KV Cache Compression for Efficient Autoregressive Video Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Autoregressive video diffusion models face scalability issues due to high attention complexity and memory overhead from redundant key-value caches, which are addressed through a hybrid compression strategy that separates attention heads into static and dynamic categories for optimized caching.  AI-generated summary Autoregressive (AR) video diffusion models adopt a streaming generation framework, enabling long-horizon video generation with real-time responsiveness, as exemplified by the Self Forcing training paradigm. However, existing AR video diffusion models still suffer from significant attention complexity and severe memory overhead due to the redundant key-value (KV) caches across historical frames, which limits scalability. In this paper, we tackle this challenge by introducing KV cache compression into autoregressive video diffusion. We observe that attention heads in mainstream AR diffusion models exhibit markedly distinct attention patterns and functional roles that remain stable across samples and denoising steps. Building on our empirical study of head-wise functional specialization, we divide the attention heads into two categories: static heads, which focus on transitions across autoregressive chunks and intra-frame fidelity, and dynamic heads, which govern inter-frame motion and consistency. We then propose Forcing-KV, a hybrid KV cache compression strategy that performs structured static pruning for static heads and dynamic pruning based on segment-wise similarity for dynamic heads. While maintaining output quality, our method achieves a generation speed of over 29 frames per second on a single NVIDIA H200 GPU along with 30% cache memory reduction, delivering up to 1.35x and 1.50x speedups on LongLive and Self Forcing at 480P resolution, and further scaling to 2.82x speedup at 1080P resolution. Code and demo videos are provided at https://zju-jiyicheng.github.io/Forcing-KV-Page.

ru.title: Гибридное сжатие кэша для масштабируемой видео-генерации
ru.desc: Статья посвящена оптимизации авторегрессивных диффузионных моделей для генерации видео путём сжатия кэша ключ-значение. Авторы обнаружили, что внимание в этих моделях распределяется неравномерно между головами нейронной сети: некоторые головы отвечают за статические характеристики кадров, а другие — за динамику между кадрами. Предложенный метод Forcing-KV использует гибридную стратегию, применяя структурированное прунинг для статических голов и динамическое прунинг на основе сходства сегментов для динамических голов. Решение достигает снижения памяти на 30%, ускорения генерации до 29 кадров в секунду и масштабируется до 2.82x ускорения при разрешении 1080P.

en.title: Optimizing Video Diffusion with Smart Cache Compression
en.desc: This paper addresses the scalability issues of autoregressive video diffusion models, which struggle with high attention complexity and memory overhead due to redundant key-value caches. The authors introduce a hybrid compression strategy called Forcing-KV, which categorizes attention heads into static and dynamic types for optimized caching. By employing structured static pruning for static heads and dynamic pruning based on segment-wise similarity for dynamic heads, the method reduces cache memory usage by 30%. The proposed approach significantly improves generation speed, achieving over 29 frames per second while maintaining output quality, demonstrating effective scalability for video generation tasks.

zh.title: 优化自回归视频生成的混合压缩策略
zh.desc: 自回归视频扩散模型在处理高复杂度注意力和冗余键值缓存时面临可扩展性问题。本文提出了一种混合压缩策略，将注意力头分为静态和动态两类，以优化缓存。通过对注意力头的功能特性进行实证研究，我们发现不同的注意力头在样本和去噪步骤中表现出明显的不同模式。我们的方法在保持输出质量的同时，实现了显著的生成速度提升和缓存内存减少。

papers.title: RAVEN: Real-time Autoregressive Video Extrapolation with Consistency-model GRPO
papers.abstract: Abstract RAVEN enables real-time video generation through causal autoregressive extrapolation with improved training alignment, while CM-GRPO enhances performance via reinforcement learning applied to consistency model sampling.  AI-generated summary Causal autoregressive video diffusion models support real-time streaming generation by extrapolating future chunks from previously generated content. Distilling such generators from high-fidelity bidirectional teachers yields competitive few-step models, yet a persistent gap between the history distributions encountered during training and those arising at inference constrains generation quality over long horizons. We introduce the Real-time Autoregressive Video Extrapolation Network (RAVEN), a training-time test framework that repacks each self rollout into an interleaved sequence of clean historical endpoints and noisy denoising states. This formulation aligns training attention with inference-time extrapolation and allows downstream chunk losses to supervise the history representations on which future predictions depend. We further propose Consistency-model Group Relative Policy Optimization (CM-GRPO), which reformulates a consistency sampling step as a conditional Gaussian transition and applies online Reinforcement Learning (RL) directly to this kernel, avoiding the Euler-Maruyama auxiliary process adopted in prior flow-model RL formulations. Experiments demonstrate that RAVEN surpasses recent causal video distillation baselines across quality, semantic, and dynamic degree evaluations, and that CM-GRPO provides further gains when combined with RAVEN.

ru.title: Выравнивание обучения и инференса для потоковой генерации видео через авторегрессивную экстраполяцию
ru.desc: В статье предлагается RAVEN - сеть для генерации видео в реальном времени, которая использует причинную авторегрессивную экстраполяцию и решает проблему несовпадения между распределениями данных на этапе обучения и инференса. Ключевая идея заключается в переупаковке данных во время обучения в последовательность чистых исторических точек и зашумленных состояний деноизирования, что выравнивает внимание модели с процессом экстраполяции на инференсе. Дополнительно предлагается CM-GRPO - метод применения reinforcement learning к процессу дискретизации consistency models путём преформулировки этапа как условного гауссовского перехода. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что RAVEN превосходит существующие базовые модели по качеству и семантической согласованности, а комбинация с CM-GRPO обеспечивает дополнительное улучшение производительности.

en.title: Real-Time Video Generation with RAVEN and CM-GRPO
en.desc: The paper introduces RAVEN, a novel framework for real-time video generation that utilizes causal autoregressive extrapolation to improve the alignment between training and inference. By reorganizing the training data into a sequence of clean and noisy states, RAVEN enhances the model's ability to predict future video frames based on past content. Additionally, the paper presents CM-GRPO, a method that applies reinforcement learning to optimize the consistency of video generation, improving the sampling process. Experiments show that RAVEN outperforms existing models in generating high-quality video content, especially over longer sequences.

zh.title: 实时视频生成的新突破
zh.desc: RAVEN是一种实时视频生成模型，通过因果自回归外推和改进的训练对齐实现。该模型能够从之前生成的内容中推测未来的视频片段，支持实时流媒体生成。我们还提出了CM-GRPO，它通过强化学习优化一致性模型的采样过程，进一步提升了生成性能。实验结果表明，RAVEN在视频质量、语义和动态度评估上超越了现有的基线模型。

papers.title: Does Synthetic Layered Design Data Benefit Layered Design Decomposition?
papers.abstract: Abstract Synthetic layered image data improves graphic design decomposition by enabling scalable training and better layer distribution control compared to traditional methods.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in image generation have made it easy to produce high-quality images. However, these outputs are inherently flattened, entangling foreground elements, background, and text within a fixed canvas. As a result, flexible post-generation editing remains challenging, revealing a clear last-mile gap toward practical usability. Existing approaches either rely on scarce proprietary layered assets or construct partially synthetic data from limited structural priors. However, both strategies face fundamental challenges in scalability. In this work, we investigate whether pure synthetic layered data can improve graphic design decomposition. We make the assumption that, in graphic design, effective decomposition does not require modeling inter-layer dependencies as precisely as in natural-image composition, since design elements are often intentionally arranged as modular and semantically separable components. Concretely, we conduct a data-centric study based on CLD baseline, which is a state-of-the-art layer decomposition framework. Based on the baseline, we construct our own synthetic dataset, SynLayers, generate textual supervision using vision language models, and automate inference inputs with VLM-predicted bounding boxes. Our study reveals three key findings: (1) even training with purely synthetic data can outperform non-scalable alternatives such as the widely used PrismLayersPro dataset, demonstrating its viability as a scalable and effective substitute; (2) performance consistently improves with increased training data scale, while gains begin to saturate at around 50K samples; and (3) synthetic data enables balanced control over layer-count distributions, avoiding the layer-count imbalance commonly observed in real-world datasets. We hope this data-centric study encourages broader adoption of synthetic data as a practical foundation for layered design editing systems.

ru.title: Синтетические данные как масштабируемое решение для разделения слоёв в графическом дизайне
ru.desc: В работе исследуется, как синтетические многослойные изображения могут улучшить декомпозицию графического дизайна. Авторы создали датасет SynLayers с синтетическими данными и использовали vision language models для автоматической генерации аннотаций и предсказания граничных рамок. Исследование показало, что обучение на чистых синтетических данных превосходит существующие подходы, основанные на редких реальных активах, и позволяет масштабировать процесс обучения. Кроме того, синтетические данные обеспечивают контролируемое распределение по количеству слоёв, решая проблему дисбаланса в реальных датасетах.

en.title: Unlocking Graphic Design with Synthetic Layered Data
en.desc: This paper explores the use of synthetic layered image data to enhance graphic design decomposition, which is the process of separating different elements in an image for easier editing. Traditional methods often struggle with scalability and rely on limited or proprietary datasets, making them less effective. The authors introduce a synthetic dataset called SynLayers and demonstrate that training with this data can outperform existing methods, particularly in terms of scalability and layer distribution control. Their findings suggest that synthetic data can provide a practical solution for improving the usability of graphic design tools.

zh.title: 合成数据助力图形设计分解
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了合成分层图像数据在图形设计分解中的应用。与传统方法相比，合成数据能够实现可扩展的训练和更好的层分布控制。研究表明，使用纯合成数据的训练效果优于一些非可扩展的替代方案，并且随着训练数据规模的增加，性能持续提升。我们希望这项研究能够促进合成数据在分层设计编辑系统中的广泛应用。

papers.title: PanoWorld: Towards Spatial Supersensing in 360^circ Panorama World
papers.abstract: Abstract PanoWorld with spherical spatial cross-attention enables panoramic reasoning by leveraging equirectangular projection structure and geometry-aware supervision.  AI-generated summary Multimodal large laboratory models (MLLMs) still struggle with spatial understanding under the dominant perspective-image paradigm, which inherits the narrow field of view of human-like perception. For navigation, robotic search, and 3D scene understanding, 360-degree panoramic sensing offers a form of supersensing by capturing the entire surrounding environment at once. However, existing MLLM pipelines typically decompose panoramas into multiple perspective views, leaving the spherical structure of equirectangular projection (ERP) largely implicit. In this paper, we study pano-native understanding, which requires an MLLM to reason over an ERP panorama as a continuous, observer-centered space. To this end, we first define the key abilities for pano-native understanding, including semantic anchoring, spherical localization, reference-frame transformation, and depth-aware 3D spatial reasoning. We then build a large-scale metadata construction pipeline that converts mixed-source ERP panoramas into geometry-aware, language-grounded, and depth-aware supervision, and instantiate these signals as capability-aligned instruction tuning data. On the model side, we introduce PanoWorld with Spherical Spatial Cross-Attention, which injects spherical geometry into the visual stream. We further construct PanoSpace-Bench, a diagnostic benchmark for evaluating ERP-native spatial reasoning. Experiments show that PanoWorld substantially outperforms both proprietary and open-source baselines on PanoSpace-Bench, H* Bench, and R2R-CE Val-Unseen benchmarks. These results demonstrate that robust panoramic reasoning requires dedicated pano-native supervision and geometry-aware model adaptation. All source code and proposed data will be publicly released.

ru.title: Панорамное рассуждение с осознанием сферической геометрии
ru.desc: Статья представляет PanoWorld — новый подход для обучения мультимодальных больших языковых моделей (MLLM) пониманию панорамных изображений в формате эквirectangular projection. Авторы определили ключевые навыки панорамного рассуждения (семантическая привязка, сферическая локализация, трансформация систем координат и 3D-рассуждение), а затем создали специализированный набор инструкций для обучения с использованием геометрически-осведомленной разметки и информации о глубине. В модель встроена сферическая пространственная кросс-внимание (spherical spatial cross-attention), которая явно учитывает геометрию сферы при обработке визуальной информации. Экспериментальные результаты показывают превосходство PanoWorld над существующими решениями на специализированном бенчмарке PanoSpace-Bench и других задачах навигации.

en.title: Revolutionizing Panoramic Reasoning with PanoWorld
en.desc: This paper introduces PanoWorld, a model designed to enhance spatial reasoning in panoramic images using spherical spatial cross-attention. It addresses the limitations of traditional multimodal large laboratory models (MLLMs) that struggle with understanding 360-degree environments by treating equirectangular projections as continuous spaces. The authors propose a new approach that includes geometry-aware supervision and specific capabilities like semantic anchoring and depth-aware reasoning. Experimental results show that PanoWorld significantly improves performance on various benchmarks, highlighting the importance of tailored supervision for effective panoramic reasoning.

zh.title: 全景推理的新视野：PanoWorld模型
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为PanoWorld的模型，利用球面空间交叉注意力机制来实现全景推理。该模型通过考虑等距矩形投影的结构和几何感知监督，增强了对360度全景的理解能力。我们定义了全景原生理解的关键能力，包括语义锚定、球面定位和深度感知的3D空间推理。实验结果表明，PanoWorld在多个基准测试中显著优于现有的模型，证明了专门的全景监督和几何感知模型适应性的重要性。

papers.title: BOOKMARKS: Efficient Active Storyline Memory for Role-playing
papers.abstract: Abstract BOOKMARKS is a search-based memory framework that improves role-playing agents by actively managing task-relevant information through structured bookmarks that capture detailed character behaviors and story elements.  AI-generated summary Memory systems are critical for role-playing agents (RPAs) to maintain long-horizon consistency. However, existing RPA memory methods (e.g., profiling) mainly rely on recurrent summarization, whose compression inevitably discards important details. To address this issue, we propose a search-based memory framework called BOOKMARKS, which actively initializes, maintains, and updates task-relevant pieces of bookmarks for the current task (e.g., character acting). A bookmark is structured as the answer to a question at a specific point in the storyline. For each current task, BOOKMARKS selects reusable existing bookmarks or initializes new ones (at storyline beginning) with useful questions. These bookmarks are then synchronized to the current story point, with their answers updated accordingly, so they can be efficiently reused in future grounding rounds. Compared with recurrent summarization, BOOKMARKS offers (1) active grounding for capturing task-specific details and (2) passive updating to avoid unnecessary computation. In implementation, BOOKMARKS supports concept, behavior, and state searches, each powered by an efficient synchronization method. BOOKMARKS significantly outperforms RPA memory baselines on 85 characters from 16 artifacts, demonstrating the effectiveness of search-based memory for RPAs.

ru.title: Структурированная память через активный поиск закладок для ролевых агентов
ru.desc: В этой работе предлагается BOOKMARKS — фреймворк на основе поиска для улучшения памяти ролевых агентов (RPA). Вместо стандартного подхода с периодической компрессией информации, метод использует структурированные закладки, содержащие ответы на специфические вопросы о персонажах и сюжете. Закладки активно инициализируются, обновляются и синхронизируются с текущей точкой повествования, что позволяет сохранять важные детали без потери информации. Экспериментальная оценка на 85 персонажах из 16 источников показала значительное превосходство предложенного подхода над существующими методами памяти для ролевых агентов.

en.title: Enhancing Role-Playing Agents with Smart Memory Management
en.desc: The paper introduces BOOKMARKS, a novel memory framework designed to enhance role-playing agents (RPAs) by managing relevant information through structured bookmarks. These bookmarks capture detailed character behaviors and story elements, allowing for better long-term consistency in storytelling. Unlike traditional methods that rely on recurrent summarization, which can lose important details, BOOKMARKS actively initializes and updates bookmarks based on the current task. This approach not only improves the efficiency of memory usage but also significantly outperforms existing RPA memory techniques in various scenarios.

zh.title: 基于搜索的记忆框架提升角色扮演代理的表现
zh.desc: BOOKMARKS是一种基于搜索的记忆框架，旨在通过结构化书签来改善角色扮演代理（RPA）的任务相关信息管理。该框架能够主动初始化、维护和更新与当前任务相关的书签，从而保持长时间的一致性。与传统的重复总结方法相比，BOOKMARKS通过有效的同步方法，捕捉任务特定的细节并避免不必要的计算。实验结果表明，BOOKMARKS在85个角色的测试中显著优于现有的RPA记忆基线，展示了基于搜索的记忆在角色扮演中的有效性。

papers.title: Learning to Build the Environment: Self-Evolving Reasoning RL via Verifiable Environment Synthesis
papers.abstract: Abstract Self-improving language models construct environments for training rather than generating data, utilizing stable solve-verify asymmetry to maintain informative rewards during learning.  AI-generated summary We pursue a vision for self-improving language models in which the model does not merely generate problems or traces to imitate, but constructs the environments that train it. In zero-data reasoning RL, this reframes self-improvement from a data-generation loop into an environment-construction loop, where each artifact is a reusable executable object that samples instances, computes references, and scores responses. Whether this vision sustains improvement hinges on a single property: the environments must exhibit stable solve--verify asymmetry, the model must be able to write an oracle once that it cannot reliably execute in natural language on fresh instances. This asymmetry takes two complementary forms. Some tasks are algorithmically hard to reason through but trivial as code: a dynamic program or graph traversal, compiled once, yields unboundedly many calibrated instances. Others are intrinsically hard to solve but easy to verify, like planted subset-sum or constraint satisfaction. Both create a durable gap between proposing and solving that the policy cannot close by gaming the verifier, and it is this gap that keeps reward informative as the learner improves. We instantiate this view in EvoEnv, a single-policy generator, solver method that synthesizes Python environments from ten seeds and admits them only after staged validation, semantic self-review, solver-relative difficulty calibration, and novelty checks. The strongest evidence comes from the already-strong regime: on Qwen3-4B-Thinking, fixed public-data RLVR and fixed hand-crafted environment RLVR reduce the average, while EvoEnv improves it from 72.4 to 74.8, a relative gain of 3.3%. Stable self-improvement, we suggest, depends not on producing more synthetic data, but on models learning to construct worlds whose difficulty stays structurally beyond their own reach.

ru.title: Самосовершенствование через конструирование миров
ru.desc: В статье предлагается новый подход к самосовершенствованию языковых моделей, где модель не просто генерирует данные для обучения, а конструирует окружения, которые её тренируют. Ключевая идея основана на принципе стабильной асимметрии между решением и проверкой: модель создаёт задачи, которые сама не может надёжно решить в естественном языке, но может легко проверить правильность ответов. Авторы реализуют этот подход в системе EvoEnv, которая синтезирует Python-окружения с задачами разной сложности, прошедшими многоэтапную валидацию и калибровку. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что такой метод обеспечивает устойчивое улучшение производительности моделей за счёт создания структурно сложных задач, а не просто увеличения объёма синтетических данных.

en.title: Building Better Worlds for Language Model Learning
en.desc: This paper discusses a new approach for self-improving language models that focuses on creating training environments instead of just generating data. It introduces the concept of zero-data reasoning reinforcement learning (RL), where the model builds environments that help it learn more effectively. The key idea is to maintain a stable solve-verify asymmetry, ensuring that the model can create challenging tasks that it cannot easily solve, thus keeping the learning process informative. The authors present EvoEnv, a method that generates and validates Python environments, demonstrating improved performance in language model training by emphasizing environment construction over data generation.

zh.title: 自我改进语言模型：构建超越自身的学习环境
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了自我改进的语言模型如何通过构建训练环境而不是仅仅生成数据来进行学习。它提出了一种新的思路，将自我改进从数据生成循环转变为环境构建循环，强调了稳定的求解-验证不对称性的重要性。通过这种方式，模型能够创建可重用的执行对象，从而在不同实例中进行有效的学习。研究表明，稳定的自我改进依赖于模型学习构建超出自身能力的世界，而不是单纯生成更多的合成数据。

papers.title: PhyMotion: Structured 3D Motion Reward for Physics-Grounded Human Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract PhyMotion introduces a physics-grounded reward system for human motion generation that evaluates kinematic plausibility, contact consistency, and dynamic feasibility to improve video quality.  AI-generated summary Generating realistic human motion is a central yet unsolved challenge in video generation. While reinforcement learning (RL)-based post-training has driven recent gains in general video quality, extending it to human motion remains bottlenecked by a reward signal that cannot reliably score motion realism. Existing video rewards primarily rely on 2D perceptual signals, without explicitly modeling the 3D body state, contact, and dynamics underlying articulated human motion, and often assign high scores to videos with floating bodies or physically implausible movements. To address this, we propose PhyMotion, a structured, fine-grained motion reward that grounds recovered 3D human trajectories in a physics simulator and evaluates motion quality along multiple dimensions of physical feasibility. Concretely, we recover SMPL body meshes from generated videos, retarget them onto a humanoid in the MuJoCo physics simulator, and evaluate the resulting motion along three axes: kinematic plausibility, contact and balance consistency, and dynamic feasibility. Each component provides a continuous and interpretable signal tied to a specific aspect of motion quality, allowing the reward to capture which aspects of motion are physically correct or violated. Experiments show that PhyMotion achieves stronger correlation with human judgments than existing reward formulations. These gains carry over to RL-based post-training, where optimizing PhyMotion leads to larger and more consistent improvements than optimizing existing rewards, improving motion realism across both autoregressive and bidirectional video generators under both automatic metrics and blind human evaluation (+68 Elo gain). Ablations show that the three axes provide complementary supervision signals, while the reward preserves overall video generation quality with only modest training overhead.

ru.title: Физика как критерий качества человеческого движения в видео
ru.desc: PhyMotion предлагает систему вознаграждения для генерации человеческого движения на видео, которая основана на физических симуляциях и оценивает плаусибильность кинематики, консистентность контактов тела с поверхностью и физическую реализуемость движения. Система восстанавливает 3D mesh тела SMPL из видео, переносит его в физический симулятор MuJoCo и вычисляет вознаграждение по трём измерениям физической корректности движения. Использование PhyMotion при обучении с подкреплением позволяет значительно улучшить качество сгенерированных видео, показывая более сильную корреляцию с оценками человека по сравнению с существующими методами оценки. Подход демонстрирует +68 Elo прироста и хорошо масштабируется как для автрегрессивных, так и для двунаправленных моделей генерации видео.

en.title: Enhancing Human Motion Realism with Physics-Grounded Rewards
en.desc: PhyMotion is a novel reward system designed to enhance the realism of AI-generated human motion in videos. It evaluates motion based on three key criteria: kinematic plausibility, contact consistency, and dynamic feasibility, ensuring that movements are physically realistic. By utilizing a physics simulator to analyze 3D human trajectories, PhyMotion provides a more reliable and interpretable reward signal compared to traditional 2D perceptual methods. Experiments demonstrate that this approach significantly improves the correlation with human judgments and enhances the quality of generated motion in reinforcement learning settings.

zh.title: 基于物理的运动生成奖励系统
zh.desc: PhyMotion提出了一种基于物理的奖励系统，用于生成逼真的人类运动。该系统通过评估运动的运动学合理性、接触一致性和动态可行性来提高视频质量。与传统的2D感知信号不同，PhyMotion明确建模了3D身体状态和运动动态，从而更准确地评分运动的真实感。实验表明，PhyMotion在与人类判断的相关性上优于现有的奖励公式，并在强化学习后训练中实现了更大的改进。

papers.title: PRISM: Prior Rectification and Uncertainty-Aware Structure Modeling for Diffusion-Based Text Image Super-Resolution
papers.abstract: Abstract PRISM is a diffusion-based text super-resolution framework that improves accuracy under severe degradation by using flow-matching prior rectification and uncertainty-aware residual encoding.  AI-generated summary Text image super-resolution (Text-SR) requires more than visually plausible detail synthesis: slight errors in stroke topology may alter character identity and break readability. Existing methods improve text fidelity with stronger recognition-based or generative priors, yet they still face two unresolved challenges under severe degradation: the text condition extracted from low-quality inputs can itself be unreliable, and a plausible global prior does not fully determine fine-grained stroke boundaries. We present PRISM, a single-step diffusion-based Text-SR framework that addresses these two challenges through Flow-Matching Prior Rectification (FMPR) and a Structure-guided Uncertainty-aware Residual Encoder (SURE). FMPR constructs a privileged training-time prior from paired low-quality/high-quality latents and learns a flow matching that transports degraded embeddings toward this restoration-oriented prior space, yielding more accurate and reliable global text guidance. SURE further predicts uncertainty-aware structural residuals to selectively absorb reliable local boundary evidence while suppressing ambiguous stroke cues. Together, these components enable explicit global prior rectification and local structure refinement within a single diffusion restoration pass. Experiments on both synthetic and real-world benchmarks show that PRISM achieves state-of-the-art performance with millisecond-level inference. Our dataset and code will be available at https://github.com/faithxuz/PRISM.

ru.title: Надёжное восстановление текста через исправление приора и неопределённость
ru.desc: PRISM — это фреймворк на основе диффузионных моделей для улучшения качества текста в изображениях низкого разрешения. Метод решает две ключевые проблемы: ненадёжность текстовых условий, извлекаемых из деградированных изображений, и неопределённость при восстановлении точных границ штрихов символов. Авторы предлагают использовать Flow-Matching Prior Rectification (FMPR) для построения надёжного глобального приора в скрытом пространстве и Structure-guided Uncertainty-aware Residual Encoder (SURE) для предсказания локальных структурных остатков с учётом неопределённости. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что PRISM достигает лучшего качества восстановления с минимальной вычислительной сложностью.

en.title: Enhancing Text Clarity with PRISM: A Diffusion Approach
en.desc: PRISM is a novel framework designed for text super-resolution (Text-SR) that enhances the clarity of text images, especially when they are severely degraded. It utilizes Flow-Matching Prior Rectification (FMPR) to create a reliable training prior that helps in accurately restoring text by aligning degraded inputs with high-quality references. Additionally, the Structure-guided Uncertainty-aware Residual Encoder (SURE) focuses on refining local stroke details while managing uncertainty, ensuring that only the most reliable features are used for reconstruction. This combination allows PRISM to effectively improve text fidelity and readability in a single diffusion step, achieving top performance in both synthetic and real-world tests.

zh.title: PRISM：提升文本超分辨率的新方法
zh.desc: PRISM是一种基于扩散的文本超分辨率框架，旨在在严重退化的情况下提高文本的准确性。它通过流匹配先验修正和不确定性感知残差编码来解决文本识别中的挑战。PRISM能够在单步扩散恢复过程中，显式地修正全局先验并细化局部结构，从而提高文本的可读性和准确性。实验结果表明，PRISM在合成和真实世界基准测试中均表现出色，达到了最先进的性能。

papers.title: Adaptive Teacher Exposure for Self-Distillation in LLM Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Adaptive Teacher Exposure for Self-Distillation (ATESD) improves large language model reasoning by dynamically adjusting teacher exposure during training through a learnable policy controller.  AI-generated summary On-policy self-distillation has become a strong recipe for LLM reasoning, where a privileged teacher supervises the student's own rollouts while conditioning on the reference solution. A design choice shared by nearly all such methods, however, has gone unquestioned: the teacher always sees the full reference reasoning. We argue that this default itself is part of the problem and identify a teacher-side exposure mismatch: when the teacher conditions on reasoning far beyond the student's current competence, the resulting token targets become too strong to absorb. A controlled fixed-exposure sweep makes this concrete on two fronts: 1) full exposure is not reliably the best choice, and 2) student-teacher mismatch grows monotonically as the teacher sees more privileged reasoning. This motivates treating teacher exposure not as a fixed hyperparameter but as a learnable training-time control variable. We therefore propose Adaptive Teacher Exposure for Self-Distillation (ATESD). ATESD models the reveal ratio with a lightweight Beta-policy controller conditioned on compact training-state statistics, and uses one sampled exposure for a short hold window of student updates. To make this exposure controller learnable, we optimize it with a discounted learning-progress reward that scores each held decision by its effect on the student's future improvement rather than its immediate loss change, addressing the delayed credit assignment induced by on-policy distillation. Experiments on AIME 24, AIME 25, and HMMT 25 across Qwen3-{1.7B, 4B, 8B} show that ATESD consistently outperforms competitive self-distillation and RL baselines, improving over OPSD by +0.95, +2.05, and +2.33 Average@12 points respectively, and establishing adaptive teacher exposure as an effective new axis for reasoning self-distillation.

ru.title: Адаптивное управление видимостью учителя для лучшего обучения студента
ru.desc: Статья предлагает адаптивный метод управления доступом учителя к эталонным решениям при обучении больших языковых моделей через самодистилляцию. Авторы выявили проблему несоответствия между учителем и студентом: когда учитель видит полное решение, которое намного превосходит текущий уровень студента, целевые токены становятся слишком сложными для усвоения. Решение заключается в использовании обучаемого контроллера на основе Beta-политики, который динамически управляет тем, какую часть решения видит учитель во время тренировки. Контроллер оптимизируется через функцию награды, основанную на прогрессе обучения студента, что решает проблему отложенного назначения кредита в on-policy дистилляции.

en.title: Dynamic Teacher Exposure for Enhanced Learning
en.desc: The paper introduces Adaptive Teacher Exposure for Self-Distillation (ATESD), a method that enhances the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) by dynamically adjusting how much information the teacher model shares during training. It identifies a problem where the teacher's full exposure to reference reasoning can overwhelm the student model, leading to ineffective learning. By treating teacher exposure as a learnable variable rather than a fixed parameter, ATESD allows for a more tailored training experience that aligns better with the student's current abilities. Experimental results demonstrate that ATESD significantly improves performance over existing self-distillation methods, highlighting its potential as a novel approach in the field.

zh.title: 自适应教师曝光，提升推理能力！
zh.desc: 自适应教师曝光（ATESD）通过动态调整训练期间的教师曝光，提升了大型语言模型的推理能力。该方法引入了一个可学习的策略控制器，以便在训练过程中根据学生的能力调整教师的监督程度。研究表明，教师在监督时看到的参考推理过多，可能导致学生无法有效吸收这些信息。通过优化教师曝光的比例，ATESD显著提高了自我蒸馏的效果，成为推理自我蒸馏的新方向。

papers.title: WildTableBench: Benchmarking Multimodal Foundation Models on Table Understanding In the Wild
papers.abstract: Abstract WildTableBench is introduced as the first question-answering benchmark for real-world table images, revealing significant challenges in structural perception and numerical reasoning for existing multimodal models.  AI-generated summary Using multimodal foundation models to analyze table images is a high-value yet challenging application in consumer and enterprise scenarios. Despite its importance, current evaluations rely largely on structured-text tables or clean rendered images, leaving the visual complexity of in-the-wild table images underexplored. Such images feature varied layouts and diverse domains that demand sophisticated structural perception and numerical reasoning. To bridge this gap, we introduce WildTableBench, the first question-answering benchmark for naturally occurring table images from real-world settings. WildTableBench comprises 402 high-information-density table images collected from online forums and websites across diverse domains, together with 928 manually annotated and verified questions spanning 17 subtypes across five categories. We evaluate 21 frontier proprietary and open-source multimodal foundation models on this benchmark. Only one model exceeds 50% accuracy, while all remaining models range from 4.1% to 49.9%. We further conduct diagnostic analyses to characterize model failures and reveal persistent weaknesses in structural perception and reasoning. These results and analyses provide useful insights into current model capabilities and establish WildTableBench as a valuable diagnostic benchmark for table image understanding.

ru.title: Когда даже лучшие модели падают перед реальными таблицами
ru.desc: В статье представлена WildTableBench — первый бенчмарк для оценки способности мультимодальных моделей отвечать на вопросы о реальных изображениях таблиц из интернета. Бенчмарк содержит 402 высокоинформативных изображения таблиц и 928 вручную аннотированных вопросов, охватывающих различные домены и типы задач. Авторы протестировали 21 современную мультимодальную модель и обнаружили, что только одна превышает 50% точности, что свидетельствует о серьёзных проблемах в восприятии структуры и числовых рассуждениях. Диагностический анализ выявил устойчивые слабости существующих моделей в понимании сложных таблиц реального мира.

en.title: WildTableBench: Tackling Real-World Table Image Challenges in AI
en.desc: WildTableBench is a new benchmark designed to test how well machine learning models can answer questions about real-world table images. These images are complex and come from various sources, making it hard for models to understand their structure and perform numerical reasoning. The benchmark includes 402 table images and 928 questions, highlighting the challenges faced by existing multimodal models. The evaluation shows that most models struggle, with only one achieving over 50% accuracy, indicating a need for improvement in understanding and reasoning with table data.

zh.title: WildTableBench：真实表格图像问答的新基准
zh.desc: WildTableBench是第一个针对真实世界表格图像的问答基准，揭示了现有多模态模型在结构感知和数值推理方面的重大挑战。该基准包含来自在线论坛和网站的402张高信息密度的表格图像，以及928个手动注释的问题，涵盖17个子类型和五个类别。我们对21个前沿的多模态基础模型进行了评估，只有一个模型的准确率超过50%，其余模型的准确率在4.1%到49.9%之间。通过这些结果和分析，我们为当前模型的能力提供了有价值的见解，并确立了WildTableBench作为表格图像理解的重要诊断基准。

papers.title: Aligning Latent Geometry for Spherical Flow Matching in Image Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Geodesic flow matching improves image generation by projecting latents onto fixed radius spheres and using spherical linear interpolation instead of linear paths, preserving semantic content through angular components.  AI-generated summary Latent flow matching for image generation usually transports Gaussian noise to variational autoencoder latents along linear paths. Both endpoints, however, concentrate in thin spherical shells, and a Euclidean chord leaves those shells even when preprocessing aligns their radii. By decomposing each latent token into radial and angular components, we show through component-swap probes that decoded perceptual and semantic content is carried predominantly by direction, with radius contributing much less. We therefore project data latents onto a fixed token radius, use the radial projection of Gaussian noise as the spherical prior, finetune the decoder with the encoder frozen, and replace linear interpolation with spherical linear interpolation. The resulting geodesic paths stay on the sphere at every timestep, and their velocity targets are purely angular by construction. Under matched training, the method consistently improves class-conditional ImageNet-256 FID across different image tokenizers, leaves the diffusion architecture unchanged, and requires no auxiliary encoder or representation-alignment objective.

ru.title: Геодезические пути на сфере для более качественной генерации изображений
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод потокового соответствия на геодезических путях для улучшения генерации изображений. Авторы показали, что семантическое содержание изображений кодируется преимущественно в угловых компонентах латентных представлений, а не в радиальных. Вместо линейной интерполяции между гауссовым шумом и латентами вариационного автокодировщика используется сферическая линейная интерполяция на сфере фиксированного радиуса. Такой подход обеспечивает последовательное улучшение метрики FID на ImageNet-256 без изменения архитектуры диффузионной модели.

en.title: Enhancing Image Generation with Geodesic Flow Matching
en.desc: This paper introduces a method called geodesic flow matching to enhance image generation in machine learning. It focuses on projecting latent variables onto fixed-radius spheres and using spherical linear interpolation instead of traditional linear paths. By separating latent tokens into radial and angular components, the authors demonstrate that the semantic content is mainly preserved through angular direction. The proposed approach improves the quality of generated images while maintaining the existing diffusion architecture and requiring no additional encoders.

zh.title: 测地线流匹配：提升图像生成质量的创新方法
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种改进图像生成的方法，称为测地线流匹配。它通过将潜在变量投影到固定半径的球面上，并使用球面线性插值来替代线性路径，从而保持语义内容。研究表明，潜在变量的解码感知和语义内容主要由方向决定，而半径的贡献较小。该方法在训练过程中能够有效提高图像生成的质量，尤其是在不同的图像标记器上。

papers.title: Topology-Preserving Neural Operator Learning via Hodge Decomposition
papers.abstract: Abstract Physical field equations on geometric meshes are analyzed through Hodge theory to develop a hybrid Eulerian-Lagrangian architecture that improves accuracy and efficiency by separating topological and geometric components.  AI-generated summary In this paper, we study solution operators of physical field equations on geometric meshes from a function-space perspective. We reveal that Hodge orthogonality fundamentally resolves spectral interference by isolating unlearnable topological degrees of freedom from learnable geometric dynamics, enabling an additive approximation confined to structure-preserving subspaces. Building on Hodge theory and operator splitting, we derive a principled operator-level decomposition. The result is a Hybrid Eulerian-Lagrangian architecture with an algebraic-level inductive bias we call Hodge Spectral Duality (HSD). In our framework, we use discrete differential forms to capture topology-dominated components and an orthogonal auxiliary ambient space to represent complex local dynamics. Our method achieves superior accuracy and efficiency on geometric graphs with enhanced fidelity to physical invariants. Our code is available at https://github.com/ContinuumCoder/Hodge-Spectral-Duality

ru.title: Разделение топологии и геометрии через спектральную двойственность Ходжа
ru.desc: В работе предложена гибридная архитектура Эйлера-Лагранжа для решения уравнений физических полей на геометрических сетках, основанная на теории Ходжа. Авторы показывают, что ортогональность Ходжа позволяет разделить топологические степени свободы, которые невозможно обучить, от геометрической динамики, которая поддаётся обучению. Метод использует дискретные дифференциальные формы для описания топологических компонентов и вспомогательное ортогональное пространство для представления локальной динамики. Предложенный подход (Hodge Spectral Duality) обеспечивает улучшенную точность и эффективность с лучшим сохранением физических инвариантов.

en.title: Enhancing Accuracy in Physical Field Equations with Hodge Spectral Duality
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to solving physical field equations using Hodge theory on geometric meshes. It introduces a Hybrid Eulerian-Lagrangian architecture that separates topological features from geometric dynamics, improving both accuracy and efficiency. By applying Hodge orthogonality, the method isolates unlearnable components, allowing for better approximations within structure-preserving subspaces. The proposed framework, termed Hodge Spectral Duality (HSD), enhances the fidelity of solutions on geometric graphs while maintaining physical invariants.

zh.title: Hodge谱对偶：提升物理场方程求解的准确性与效率
zh.desc: 本文通过Hodge理论分析几何网格上的物理场方程，提出了一种混合的欧拉-拉格朗日架构。这种架构通过分离拓扑和几何成分，提高了计算的准确性和效率。我们揭示了Hodge正交性如何有效地隔离不可学习的拓扑自由度与可学习的几何动态，从而实现结构保持的子空间中的加法近似。最终，我们的方法在几何图上实现了更高的准确性和效率，同时更好地保持了物理不变性。

papers.title: Sat3DGen: Comprehensive Street-Level 3D Scene Generation from Single Satellite Image
papers.abstract: Abstract Sat3DGen addresses the challenge of generating street-level 3D scenes from satellite images by employing a geometry-first approach that improves both geometric accuracy and photorealism through novel constraints and training strategies.  AI-generated summary Generating a street-level 3D scene from a single satellite image is a crucial yet challenging task. Current methods present a stark trade-off: geometry-colorization models achieve high geometric fidelity but are typically building-focused and lack semantic diversity. In contrast, proxy-based models use feed-forward image-to-3D frameworks to generate holistic scenes by jointly learning geometry and texture, a process that yields rich content but coarse and unstable geometry. We attribute these geometric failures to the extreme viewpoint gap and sparse, inconsistent supervision inherent in satellite-to-street data. We introduce Sat3DGen to address these fundamental challenges, which embodies a geometry-first methodology. This methodology enhances the feed-forward paradigm by integrating novel geometric constraints with a perspective-view training strategy, explicitly countering the primary sources of geometric error. This geometry-centric strategy yields a dramatic leap in both 3D accuracy and photorealism. For validation, we first constructed a new benchmark by pairing the VIGOR-OOD test set with high-resolution DSM data. On this benchmark, our method improves geometric RMSE from 6.76m to 5.20m. Crucially, this geometric leap also boosts photorealism, reducing the Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) from sim40 to 19 against the leading method, Sat2Density++, despite using no extra tailored image-quality modules. We demonstrate the versatility of our high-quality 3D assets through diverse downstream applications, including semantic-map-to-3D synthesis, multi-camera video generation, large-scale meshing, and unsupervised single-image Digital Surface Model (DSM) estimation. The code has been released on https://github.com/qianmingduowan/Sat3DGen.
affiliations: LIESMARS & School of Artificial Intelligence, Wuhan University

ru.title: От спутника к улице: геометрия прежде всего в синтезе 3D сцен
ru.desc: Sat3DGen решает проблему генерации 3D сцен уровня улицы из спутниковых снимков, используя подход, ориентированный на геометрию. Метод интегрирует новые геометрические ограничения и стратегию обучения с перспективным видом для преодоления экстремальных различий в точках обзора между спутником и землёй. Авторы предлагают geometry-first методологию, которая значительно улучшает как геометрическую точность (снижая RMSE с 6.76м до 5.20м), так и фотореалистичность (уменьшая FID с 40 до 19). Полученные высококачественные 3D активы демонстрируют универсальность и применяются в задачах синтеза семантических карт, генерации видео с множественными камерами и оценке цифровых моделей высот.

en.title: Transforming Satellite Images into Accurate 3D Street Scenes
en.desc: Sat3DGen is a novel approach for generating street-level 3D scenes from satellite images, focusing on improving both geometric accuracy and photorealism. It introduces a geometry-first methodology that integrates new geometric constraints and a perspective-view training strategy to address common issues in existing models. By doing so, it significantly reduces geometric errors and enhances the quality of generated scenes. The method has been validated against a new benchmark, showing substantial improvements in both geometric RMSE and photorealism metrics compared to previous techniques.

zh.title: 以几何为中心，提升街景 3D 生成的真实感与准确性
zh.desc: Sat3DGen 是一种新方法，旨在从卫星图像生成街景 3D 场景。它采用以几何为中心的方法，通过引入新颖的几何约束和训练策略，显著提高了几何准确性和真实感。与传统方法相比，Sat3DGen 能够更好地处理视角差异和稀疏监督问题，从而减少几何误差。实验结果表明，该方法在 3D 准确性和真实感方面都有显著提升，适用于多种下游应用。

papers.title: Dynamic Latent Routing
papers.abstract: Abstract Temporal composition of sub-policies in MDPs with time-varying rewards enables optimal policy recovery through generalized Dijkstra search, which inspires a dynamic latent routing method for language model fine-tuning that outperforms traditional supervised approaches.  AI-generated summary We investigate the temporal concatenation of sub-policies in Markov Decision Processes (MDP) with time-varying reward functions. We introduce General Dijkstra Search (GDS), and prove that globally optimal goal-reaching policies can be recovered through temporal composition of intermediate optimal sub-policies. Motivated by the "search, select, update" principle underlying GDS, we propose Dynamic Latent Routing (DLR), a language-model post-training method that jointly learns discrete latent codes, routing policies, and model parameters through dynamic search in a single training stage. In low-data fine-tuning settings, DLR matches or outperforms supervised fine-tuning across four datasets and six models, achieving a mean gain of +6.6 percentage points, while prior discrete-latent baselines consistently underperform SFT. Mechanistic analyses and targeted code ablations show that DLR learns structured routing behaviors with distinct causal roles.

ru.title: Динамическая маршрутизация через оптимальную композицию под-политик
ru.desc: Работа исследует временную композицию под-политик в марковских процессах решений с изменяющимися во времени функциями вознаграждения. Авторы предлагают метод обобщённого поиска Дейкстры, который гарантирует восстановление глобально оптимальных политик через комбинацию промежуточных оптимальных под-политик. На основе этого подхода разработан метод Dynamic Latent Routing для дообучения языковых моделей, который совместно обучает дискретные латентные коды, политики маршрутизации и параметры модели. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что метод превосходит стандартное supervised fine-tuning на 6,6 процентных пункта в условиях ограниченного количества данных.

en.title: Dynamic Routing for Enhanced Language Model Fine-Tuning
en.desc: This paper explores how to combine smaller decision-making strategies, called sub-policies, in Markov Decision Processes (MDPs) that have changing rewards over time. It introduces a method called General Dijkstra Search (GDS) that can find the best overall strategy by piecing together these smaller strategies. The authors then present Dynamic Latent Routing (DLR), a new approach for fine-tuning language models that learns to select and update information dynamically during training. DLR shows significant improvements in performance compared to traditional supervised methods, especially in situations with limited data.

zh.title: 动态潜在路由：超越传统微调的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文研究了在具有时间变化奖励的马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）中，子策略的时间组合。我们引入了广义Dijkstra搜索（GDS），证明通过中间最优子策略的时间组合可以恢复全局最优的目标达成策略。基于GDS的“搜索、选择、更新”原则，我们提出了动态潜在路由（DLR），这是一种语言模型后训练方法，可以在单一训练阶段中联合学习离散潜在编码、路由策略和模型参数。在低数据微调设置中，DLR在四个数据集和六个模型上与传统的监督微调相匹配或超越，平均提升6.6个百分点。

papers.title: LLM-based Detection of Manipulative Political Narratives
papers.abstract: Abstract A computational framework combining prompt-based filtering and unsupervised clustering identifies manipulative political narrative clusters from social media posts without requiring predefined categories.  AI-generated summary We present a new computational framework for detecting and structuring manipulative political narratives. A task that became more important due to the shift of political discussions to social media. One of the primary challenges thereby is differentiating between manipulative political narratives and legitimate critiques. Some posts may also reframe actual events within a manipulative context.   To achieve good clustering results, we filter manipulative posts beforehand using a detailed few-shot prompt that combines documented campaign narratives with legitimate criticisms to differentiate them. This prompt enables a reasoning model to assign labels, retaining only manipulative narrative posts for further processing.   The remaining posts are subsequently embedded and dimensionality-reduced using UMAP, before HDBSCAN is applied to uncover narrative groups. A key advantage of this unsupervised approach is its independence from a predefined list of target categories, enabling it to uncover new narrative clusters.   Finally, a reasoning model is employed to uncover the narrative behind each cluster. This approach, applied to over 1.2 million social media posts, effectively identified 41 distinct manipulative narrative clusters by integrating prompt-based filtering with unsupervised clustering.

ru.title: Обнаружение манипулятивных политических нарративов через фильтрацию и неконтролируемую кластеризацию
ru.desc: В работе предложена вычислительная система для выявления манипулятивных политических нарративов в социальных сетях. Сначала используется few-shot prompt с документированными кампаниями для фильтрации манипулятивных постов, отделяя их от легитимной критики. Затем отфильтрованные посты встраиваются в векторное пространство, подвергаются редукции размерности через UMAP и группируются с помощью HDBSCAN для обнаружения кластеров нарративов. Преимущество этого подхода состоит в том, что он работает без предопределённых категорий, позволяя обнаруживать новые типы манипулятивных нарративов автоматически.

en.title: Uncovering Manipulative Narratives in Social Media
en.desc: This paper introduces a novel computational framework that combines prompt-based filtering with unsupervised clustering to identify manipulative political narratives in social media posts. The framework first uses a few-shot prompt to filter out legitimate critiques from manipulative posts, allowing for more accurate clustering. After filtering, the remaining posts are embedded and reduced in dimensionality using UMAP, followed by the application of HDBSCAN to discover narrative clusters. This unsupervised method does not rely on predefined categories, enabling the identification of new narrative groups from over 1.2 million posts, resulting in 41 distinct manipulative narrative clusters.

zh.title: 揭示操控性政治叙事的新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的计算框架，用于从社交媒体帖子中识别和结构化操控性的政治叙事。该框架结合了基于提示的过滤和无监督聚类，能够在没有预定义类别的情况下发现操控性叙事集群。通过使用详细的少量示例提示，模型能够区分操控性帖子和合法批评，从而提高聚类效果。最终，该方法成功识别出41个不同的操控性叙事集群，展示了其在处理社交媒体数据中的有效性。

papers.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract FutureSim enables evaluation of AI agents' long-term predictive capabilities by simulating chronological real-world event sequences, revealing significant gaps in current forecasting performance.  AI-generated summary AI agents are being increasingly deployed in dynamic, open-ended environments that require adapting to new information as it arrives. To efficiently measure this capability for realistic use-cases, we propose building grounded simulations that replay real-world events in the order they occurred. We build FutureSim, where agents forecast world events beyond their knowledge cutoff while interacting with a chronological replay of the world: real news articles arriving and questions resolving over the simulated period. We evaluate frontier agents in their native harness, testing their ability to predict world events over a three-month period from January to March 2026. FutureSim reveals a clear separation in their capabilities, with the best agent's accuracy being 25%, and many having worse Brier skill score than making no prediction at all. Through careful ablations, we show how FutureSim offers a realistic setting to study emerging research directions like long-horizon test-time adaptation, search, memory, and reasoning about uncertainty. Overall, we hope our benchmark design paves the way to measure AI progress on open-ended adaptation spanning long time-horizons in the real world.
affiliations: Institute for AI, University of Stuttgart
Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems

ru.title: Мерилка способности AI предвидеть будущее в реальном времени
ru.desc: FutureSim — это бенчмарк для оценки способности AI агентов к долгосрочному прогнозированию мировых событий. Система моделирует реальные события в хронологическом порядке, позволяя агентам делать предсказания на три месяца вперёд, получая обновления из новостных источников. Тестирование передовых моделей выявило значительный разрыв в производительности: лучший агент достигал только 25% точности, а многие показывали результаты хуже случайного предсказания по метрике Brier skill score. Бенчмарк открывает возможности для исследования адаптации в реальном времени, поиска оптимальных решений и работы с неопределённостью в долгосрочных горизонтах предсказания.

en.title: FutureSim: Bridging the Gap in AI Predictive Capabilities
en.desc: FutureSim is a tool designed to evaluate how well AI agents can predict future events based on past real-world data. It simulates a sequence of events, allowing agents to interact with real news articles and answer questions as they unfold over time. The study shows that even the best AI agents only achieved a 25% accuracy in their predictions, highlighting significant limitations in their forecasting abilities. FutureSim aims to provide a realistic framework for researching how AI can adapt and improve its predictive skills in dynamic environments.

zh.title: FutureSim：评估AI长期预测能力的新工具
zh.desc: FutureSim 是一个用于评估人工智能代理长期预测能力的工具，通过模拟现实世界事件的时间顺序，揭示了当前预测性能的显著差距。该系统允许 AI 代理在动态环境中适应新信息，并通过回放真实事件来进行预测。我们对前沿代理进行了评估，发现最佳代理的准确率仅为 25%，许多代理的表现甚至不如随机预测。通过细致的实验，我们展示了 FutureSim 如何为研究长时间适应、搜索、记忆和不确定性推理等新兴方向提供现实的环境。

papers.title: Ideology Prediction of German Political Texts
papers.abstract: Abstract A transformer-based model projects political orientation on a continuous spectrum using multiple corpora, achieving high accuracy in detecting political bias across different text sources.  AI-generated summary Elections represent a crucial milestone in a nation's ongoing development. To better understand the political rhetoric from various movements, ranging from left to right, we propose a transformer-based model capable of projecting the political orientation of a text on a continuous left-to-right spectrum, represented by a normalized scalar d between -1 and 1. This approach enables analysts to focus on specific segments of the political landscape, such as conservatives, while excluding liberal and far-right movements. Such a task can only be achieved with multiclass classifiers, provided that the desired orientation is incorporated within one of their predefined classes. To determine the most suitable foundation model among 13 candidate transformers for this task, we constructed four distinct corpora. One corpus comprised annotated plenary notes from the German Bundestag, while another was based on an official online decision-making tool, Wahl-O-Mat. The third corpus consisted of articles from 33 newspapers, each identified by its political orientation, and the fourth included 535,200 tweets from 597 members of the 20th and 21st German Bundestag. To mitigate overfitting, we used two distinct corpora for training and two for testing, respectively. For in-domain performance, DeBERTa-large achieved the highest F1 score F1=0.844 as well as for the X (Twitter) out-of-domain test ACC=0.864. Regarding the newspaper out-of-domain test, Gemma2-2B excelled (MAE = 0.172). This study demonstrates that transformer models can recognize political framing in German news at the level of public opinion polls. Our findings suggest that both the model architecture and the availability of domain-specific training data can be as influential as model size for estimating political bias. We discuss methodological limitations and outline directions for improving the robustness of bias measurement.
affiliations: Bundeswehr University Munich Research Institute CODE

ru.title: Трансформеры для измерения политического смещения на непрерывной шкале
ru.desc: Авторы разработали трансформер-модель для определения политической ориентации текстов на непрерывной шкале от -1 до 1, позволяющую анализировать политический уклон различных источников. Для обучения использовались четыре корпуса: стенограммы Бундестага, данные инструмента Wahl-O-Mat, статьи из 33 газет и твиты депутатов. Модель DeBERTa-large показала лучший результат на тестовых данных из одного домена (F1=0.844), а Gemma2-2B продемонстрировала наилучшую производительность на газетных текстах. Исследование подтверждает, что архитектура модели и качество специализированного обучающего набора данных могут быть столь же важны для определения политического смещения, как и размер параметров модели.

en.title: Transforming Political Bias Detection with Continuous Spectrum Analysis
en.desc: This paper presents a transformer-based model that measures political orientation on a continuous scale from left to right, using various text sources. The model utilizes multiclass classifiers to categorize political bias, allowing analysts to focus on specific political segments. It evaluates 13 transformer models across four distinct corpora, including parliamentary notes and tweets, to determine the best performance in detecting political bias. The results indicate that model architecture and domain-specific training data significantly impact the accuracy of political bias estimation.

zh.title: 变换器模型揭示政治倾向的连续光谱
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种基于变换器的模型，能够将文本的政治倾向映射到一个连续的左-右光谱上。通过使用多个语料库，该模型在检测不同文本来源的政治偏见方面取得了高准确率。我们构建了四个不同的语料库，包括德国联邦议会的记录和来自报纸及社交媒体的文章，以评估不同变换器模型的表现。研究结果表明，模型架构和领域特定的训练数据对政治偏见的估计同样重要。

papers.title: Boosting Omni-Modal Language Models: Staged Post-Training with Visually Debiased Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract Research demonstrates that current omni-modal benchmarks may inflate performance through visual shortcuts, and shows that post-training techniques can improve model performance on a cleaned benchmark with reduced visual leakage.  AI-generated summary Omni-modal language models are intended to jointly understand audio, visual inputs, and language, but benchmark gains can be inflated when visual evidence alone is enough to answer a query. We study whether current omni-modal benchmarks separate visual shortcuts from genuine audio-visual-language evidence integration, and how post-training behaves under a visually debiased evaluation setting. We audit nine omni-modal benchmarks with visual-only probing, remove visually solvable queries, and retain full subsets when filtering is undefined or would make comparisons unstable. This yields OmniClean, a cleaned evaluation view with 8,551 retained queries from 16,968 audited queries. On OmniClean, we evaluate OmniBoost, a three-stage post-training recipe based on Qwen2.5-Omni-3B: mixed bi-modal SFT, mixed-modality RLVR, and SFT on self-distilled data. Balanced bi-modal SFT gives limited and uneven gains, RLVR provides the first broad improvement, and self-distillation reshapes the benchmark profile. After SFT on self-distilled data, the 3B model reaches performance comparable to, and in aggregate slightly above, Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct without using a stronger omni-modal teacher. These results show that omni-modal progress is easier to interpret when evaluation controls visual leakage, and that small omni-modal models can benefit from staged post-training with self-distilled omni-query supervision. Project page: https://cheliu-computation.github.io/omni/

ru.title: Чистое мультимодальное понимание: избежание визуальных ярлыков через самодистилляцию
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема визуальных ярлыков в мультимодальных бенчмарках, которые завышают качество моделей, позволяя им отвечать на основе только визуальной информации. Авторы создали OmniClean — очищенный набор данных из 8,551 запросов, удалив визуально решаемые примеры из 16,968 аудированных запросов девяти существующих бенчмарков. На основе этого набора они разработали OmniBoost — рецепт постобучения с тремя этапами: смешанная двумодальная SFT, RLVR и самодистилляция данных. Результаты показывают, что небольшие мультимодальные модели при правильной методологии обучения могут достичь производительности, сравнимой с моделями в 10 раз большего размера.

en.title: Enhancing Omni-Modal Models by Reducing Visual Shortcuts
en.desc: This paper investigates the effectiveness of omni-modal language models, which are designed to process audio, visual, and textual data together. It highlights that existing benchmarks may overestimate model performance due to reliance on visual shortcuts, where visual information alone can answer questions. The authors introduce OmniClean, a refined evaluation set that filters out visually solvable queries, allowing for a more accurate assessment of model capabilities. They also present OmniBoost, a post-training strategy that enhances model performance through a structured approach, demonstrating that smaller models can achieve competitive results with proper training techniques.

zh.title: 清理基准，提升全模态模型性能
zh.desc: 本研究表明，当前的全模态基准可能通过视觉捷径夸大模型性能。我们提出了一种后训练技术，可以在减少视觉泄漏的清理基准上提高模型性能。我们审计了九个全模态基准，去除了仅依赖视觉的查询，保留了有效的查询集合。最终，我们的实验结果显示，经过自蒸馏数据的后训练，模型性能达到了与更大模型相当的水平。

papers.title: RewardHarness: Self-Evolving Agentic Post-Training
papers.abstract: Abstract RewardHarness is a self-evolving framework that improves image edit evaluation by iteratively developing tools and skills from limited human demonstrations, achieving superior performance compared to existing models.  AI-generated summary Evaluating instruction-guided image edits requires rewards that reflect subtle human preferences, yet current reward models typically depend on large-scale preference annotation and additional model training. This creates a data-efficiency gap: humans can often infer the target evaluation criteria from only a few examples, while models are usually trained on hundreds of thousands of comparisons. We present RewardHarness, a self-evolving agentic reward framework that reframes reward modeling as context evolution rather than weight optimization. Instead of learning from large-scale annotations, RewardHarness aligns with human preferences by iteratively evolving a library of tools and skills from as few as 100 preference demonstrations. Given a source image, candidate edited images, and an editing instruction, an Orchestrator selects the most relevant subset of tools and skills from the maintained library, and a frozen Sub-Agent uses them to construct a reasoning chain that produces a preference judgment. By comparing predicted judgments with ground-truth preferences and analyzing successes and failures in the reasoning process, the Orchestrator automatically refines its library of tools and skills without additional human annotation. Using only 0.05% of the EditReward preference data, RewardHarness achieves 47.4% average accuracy on image-editing evaluation benchmarks, surpassing GPT-5 by 5.3 points. When used as a reward signal for GRPO fine-tuning, RL-tuned models achieve 3.52 on ImgEdit-Bench. Project page: https://rewardharness.com.

ru.title: Самообучающаяся система оценки редакторских операций с минимальными примерами
ru.desc: RewardHarness — это самоэволюционирующая система для оценки качества отредактированных изображений, которая работает как агент с динамической библиотекой инструментов и навыков. Вместо традиционного обучения модели на больших объёмах данных о предпочтениях, система улучшает себя итеративно, анализируя успехи и ошибки в цепочках рассуждений на основе всего лишь сотни примеров. Оркестратор автоматически отбирает релевантные инструменты и навыки из поддерживаемой библиотеки, позволяя замороженному подагенту генерировать суждения о предпочтениях без переобучения моделей. Несмотря на использование всего 0,05% данных от существующих наборов, система достигает лучшей точности, чем GPT-4, и эффективно работает при использовании в качестве reward-сигнала для обучения с подкреплением.

en.title: Self-Evolving Framework for Efficient Image Edit Evaluation
en.desc: RewardHarness is a novel framework designed to enhance the evaluation of image edits by learning from a limited number of human demonstrations. Unlike traditional reward models that require extensive preference annotations, RewardHarness evolves its evaluation tools and skills through a self-improving process. It utilizes a library of tools to assess candidate images based on human-like preferences, refining its capabilities without needing additional human input. This approach allows RewardHarness to achieve high accuracy in image-editing evaluations with significantly less data compared to existing models.

zh.title: 自我进化的图像编辑评估框架
zh.desc: RewardHarness 是一个自我进化的框架，通过有限的人类示例逐步发展工具和技能，从而改善图像编辑评估。与传统的奖励模型依赖于大规模的偏好注释不同，RewardHarness 通过迭代演变工具库来对齐人类偏好。该框架能够在仅使用 100 个偏好示例的情况下，自动优化其工具和技能库。最终，RewardHarness 在图像编辑评估基准上取得了优于现有模型的表现，展示了其在数据效率上的优势。

papers.title: Boosting Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards via Randomly Selected Few-Shot Guidance
papers.abstract: Abstract FEST is a few-shot demonstration-guided reinforcement learning algorithm that achieves strong performance with minimal supervised fine-tuning data by combining supervised signals, on-policy learning, and weighted training to prevent overfitting.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has achieved great success in developing Large Language Models (LLMs) with chain-of-thought rollouts for many tasks such as math and coding. Nevertheless, RLVR struggles with sample efficiency on difficult problems where correct rollouts are hard to generate. Prior works propose to address this issue via demonstration-guided RLVR, i.e., to conduct Supervised FineTuning (SFT) when RL fails; however, SFT often requires a lot of data, which can be expensive to acquire. In this paper, we propose FEST, a FEw-ShoT demonstration-guided RLVR algorithm. It attains compelling results with only 128 demonstrations randomly selected from an SFT dataset. We find that three components are vital for the success: supervised signal, on-policy signal, and decaying weights on the few-shot SFT dataset to prevent overfitting from multiple-epoch training. On several benchmarks, FEST outperforms baselines with magnitudes less SFT data, even matching their performance with full dataset.

ru.title: Обучение с подкреплением на редких примерах без дорогостоящих данных
ru.desc: FEST — это алгоритм обучения с подкреплением, ориентированный на примеры, который достигает высокой производительности больших языковых моделей при минимальном объеме размеченных данных. Метод комбинирует три ключевых компонента: сигналы контролируемого обучения, обучение на текущей политике и взвешенную регуляризацию для предотвращения переобучения. Алгоритм демонстрирует, что всего 128 демонстраций достаточно для достижения результатов, сравнимых с обучением на полном наборе данных. Данный подход значительно снижает требования к количеству аннотированных примеров, что важно для практического применения обучения с подкреплением на сложных задачах.

en.title: FEST: Efficient Learning with Minimal Data
en.desc: FEST is a novel few-shot demonstration-guided reinforcement learning algorithm designed to enhance performance while minimizing the need for extensive supervised fine-tuning data. It effectively integrates supervised signals, on-policy learning, and a weighted training approach to mitigate overfitting during the learning process. By utilizing only 128 demonstrations from a larger dataset, FEST demonstrates significant improvements in sample efficiency and overall results on various benchmarks. This method allows for achieving comparable performance to traditional approaches that rely on much larger datasets, showcasing its effectiveness in challenging tasks.

zh.title: FEST：少量示范引导的强化学习新方法
zh.desc: FEST是一种基于少量示范的强化学习算法，旨在通过结合监督信号、在线学习和加权训练来防止过拟合，从而在最小的监督微调数据下实现强大的性能。该算法在处理复杂问题时表现出色，仅需从监督微调数据集中随机选择128个示范。FEST的成功依赖于三个关键组件：监督信号、在线信号和对少量示范数据集的衰减权重。通过这些方法，FEST在多个基准测试中超越了传统方法，甚至在使用完整数据集时也能匹配其性能。

papers.title: BEAM: Binary Expert Activation Masking for Dynamic Routing in MoE
papers.abstract: Abstract BEAM enables dynamic expert selection in Mixture-of-Experts models through trainable binary masks, achieving significant computational savings while maintaining high performance.
