AI-generated summary Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures enhance the efficiency of large language models by activating only a subset of experts per token. However, standard MoE employs a fixed Top-K routing strategy, leading to redundant computation and suboptimal inference latency. Existing acceleration methods either require costly retraining with architectural changes or suffer from severe performance drop at high sparsity due to train-inference mismatch. To address these limitations, we propose BEAM (Binary Expert Activation Masking), a novel method that learns token-adaptive expert selection via trainable binary masks. With a straight-through estimator and an auxiliary regularization loss, BEAM induces dynamic expert sparsity through end-to-end training while maintaining model capability. We further implement an efficient custom CUDA kernel for BEAM, ensuring seamless integration with the vLLM inference framework. Experiments show that BEAM retains over 98\% of the original model's performance while reducing MoE layer FLOPs by up to 85\%, achieving up to 2.5times faster decoding and 1.4times higher throughput, demonstrating its effectiveness as a practical, plug-and-play solution for efficient MoE inference.

ru.title: Динамический выбор экспертов для эффективного инференса больших языковых моделей
ru.desc: BEAM — это метод для оптимизации моделей Mixture-of-Experts, который использует обучаемые бинарные маски для динамического выбора экспертов в зависимости от входных токенов. Метод применяет straight-through estimator и вспомогательную функцию потерь, чтобы достичь динамической разреженности экспертов без потери качества модели. Реализация включает эффективное CUDA ядро, позволяющее интегрироваться с фреймворком vLLM для инференса. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что BEAM сохраняет 98% производительности исходной модели при снижении вычислений на 85% и ускорении декодирования в 2.5 раза.

en.title: Dynamic Expert Selection for Efficient MoE Models
en.desc: The paper introduces BEAM, a method that improves Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models by using trainable binary masks for dynamic expert selection. This approach allows the model to activate only a subset of experts for each token, significantly reducing computational costs while preserving high performance. Unlike traditional methods that rely on fixed routing strategies, BEAM adapts expert selection during training, avoiding the need for costly retraining or performance drops at high sparsity. The implementation of a custom CUDA kernel further enhances the efficiency of BEAM, making it a practical solution for faster and more effective MoE inference.

zh.title: BEAM：高效的动态专家选择解决方案
zh.desc: BEAM是一种新方法，能够通过可训练的二进制掩码实现混合专家模型中的动态专家选择，从而显著节省计算资源，同时保持高性能。传统的混合专家架构在每个token上只激活一部分专家，但固定的Top-K路由策略导致了冗余计算和不理想的推理延迟。BEAM通过学习token自适应的专家选择，解决了高稀疏性下的性能下降问题，并且在端到端训练中保持了模型的能力。实验表明，BEAM在减少MoE层FLOPs的同时，保留了超过98%的原始模型性能，显著提高了解码速度和吞吐量。

papers.title: LiSA: Lifelong Safety Adaptation via Conservative Policy Induction
papers.abstract: Abstract LiSA enables adaptive safety guardrails for AI agents by converting occasional failures into reusable policy abstractions and using evidence-aware confidence gating to improve performance under sparse and noisy feedback conditions.  AI-generated summary As AI agents move from chat interfaces to systems that read private data, call tools, and execute multi-step workflows, guardrails become a last line of defense against concrete deployment harms. In these settings, guardrail failures are no longer merely answer-quality errors: they can leak secrets, authorize unsafe actions, or block legitimate work. The hardest failures are often contextual: whether an action is acceptable depends on local privacy norms, organizational policies, and user expectations that resist pre-deployment specification. This creates a practical gap: guardrails must adapt to their own operating environments, yet deployment feedback is typically limited to sparse, noisy user-reported failures, and repeated fine-tuning is often impractical. To address this gap, we propose LiSA (Lifelong Safety Adaptation), a conservative policy induction framework that improves a fixed base guardrail through structured memory. LiSA converts occasional failures into reusable policy abstractions so that sparse reports can generalize beyond individual cases, adds conflict-aware local rules to prevent overgeneralization in mixed-label contexts, and applies evidence-aware confidence gating via a posterior lower bound, so that memory reuse scales with accumulated evidence rather than empirical accuracy alone. Across PrivacyLens+, ConFaide+, and AgentHarm, LiSA consistently outperforms strong memory-based baselines under sparse feedback, remains robust under noisy user feedback even at 20% label-flip rates, and pushes the latency--performance frontier beyond backbone model scaling. Ultimately, LiSA offers a practical path to secure AI agents against the unpredictable long tail of real-world edge risks.

ru.title: Безопасность через адаптивную память: долгосрочная защита AI-агентов
ru.desc: LiSA — это фреймворк для адаптивной безопасности AI-агентов, который преобразует редкие ошибки в переиспользуемые абстракции политик через структурированную память. Метод решает проблему контекстно-зависимых сбоев, которые зависят от локальных норм приватности и организационных политик, применяя консервативное обучение с минимальной обратной связью. LiSA использует порог уверенности, основанный на апостериорной нижней границе, чтобы обобщать редкие отчеты об ошибках без переобучения. Система демонстрирует устойчивость к шуму в размеченных данных и превосходит базовые подходы на основе памяти при разреженной обратной связи.

en.title: Adaptive Safety for AI: Learning from Failures
en.desc: LiSA (Lifelong Safety Adaptation) is a framework designed to enhance the safety of AI agents by learning from their occasional failures. It transforms these failures into reusable policy abstractions, allowing the system to adapt and improve even when feedback is sparse and noisy. By incorporating conflict-aware local rules and evidence-aware confidence gating, LiSA ensures that the guardrails can generalize effectively without overgeneralizing in complex contexts. This approach not only boosts performance under challenging conditions but also provides a practical solution for securing AI agents against unpredictable real-world risks.

zh.title: LiSA：智能代理的安全护栏新方案
zh.desc: LiSA（终身安全适应）是一种保守的策略诱导框架，旨在通过结构化记忆来改进固定的安全护栏。它将偶尔的失败转化为可重用的策略抽象，从而使稀疏的反馈报告能够超越个别案例进行推广。LiSA还引入了冲突感知的本地规则，以防止在混合标签上下文中出现过度泛化，并通过后验下界应用证据感知的置信度门控。通过在多个测试环境中表现优异，LiSA为保护AI代理提供了一条实用的路径，以应对现实世界中不可预测的边缘风险。

papers.title: Unlocking Complex Visual Generation via Closed-Loop Verified Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract A closed-loop visual reasoning framework integrates visual-language planning with diffusion generation to improve complex image synthesis while addressing latency and optimization challenges.  AI-generated summary Despite rapid advancements, current text-to-image (T2I) models predominantly rely on a single-step generation paradigm, which struggles with complex semantics and faces diminishing returns from parameter scaling.   While recent multi-step reasoning approaches show promise, they are hindered by ungrounded planning hallucinations lacking verification, monolithic post-hoc reflection, long-context optimization instabilities, and prohibitive inference latency. To overcome these bottlenecks, we propose the Closed-Loop Visual Reasoning (CLVR) framework, a comprehensive system that deeply couples visual-language logical planning with pixel-level diffusion generation. CLVR introduces an automated data engine with step-level visual verification to synthesize reliable reasoning trajectories, and proposes Proxy Prompt Reinforcement Learning (PPRL) to resolve long-context optimization instabilities by distilling interleaved multimodal histories into explicit reward signals for accurate causal attribution. Furthermore, to mitigate the severe latency bottleneck caused by iterative denoising, we propose Δ-Space Weight Merge (DSWM), a theoretically grounded method that fuses alignment weights with off-the-shelf distillation priors, reducing the per-step inference cost to just 4 NFEs without requiring expensive re-distillation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CLVR outperforms existing open-source baselines across multiple benchmarks and approaches the performance of proprietary commercial models, unlocking general test-time scaling capabilities for complex visual generation.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China (USTC)

ru.title: Замкнутый цикл визуального рассуждения: от планирования к быстрой генерации изображений
ru.desc: Статья представляет фреймворк CLVR, который объединяет визуально-языковое планирование с диффузионной генерацией для улучшения синтеза сложных изображений. Авторы предлагают автоматизированный движок данных с пошаговой визуальной верификацией для создания надежных траекторий рассуждений, а также метод Proxy Prompt Reinforcement Learning для стабилизации оптимизации при обработке длинных контекстов. Для снижения латентности итеративного денойзинга предложен метод Δ-Space Weight Merge, который объединяет веса выравнивания с приоритетами дистилляции, сокращая вычислительные затраты до 4 NFE. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что CLVR превосходит существующие открытые базовые модели и приближается к производительности коммерческих решений.

en.title: Revolutionizing Image Synthesis with Closed-Loop Visual Reasoning
en.desc: The paper presents the Closed-Loop Visual Reasoning (CLVR) framework, which enhances image synthesis by integrating visual-language planning with diffusion generation. It addresses limitations of current text-to-image models, particularly in handling complex semantics and optimizing performance. CLVR employs an automated data engine for visual verification and introduces Proxy Prompt Reinforcement Learning (PPRL) to improve long-context optimization. Additionally, it features a novel method called Δ-Space Weight Merge (DSWM) to reduce inference latency, allowing for efficient and reliable image generation.

zh.title: 闭环视觉推理：提升复杂图像合成的创新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种闭环视觉推理框架（CLVR），旨在通过将视觉语言规划与扩散生成相结合，提升复杂图像合成的效果。当前的文本到图像模型在处理复杂语义时面临挑战，且单步生成方法的效果逐渐减弱。CLVR通过引入自动化数据引擎和逐步视觉验证，解决了推理过程中的不稳定性和延迟问题。实验结果表明，CLVR在多个基准测试中超越了现有的开源模型，接近商业模型的性能。

papers.title: Nexus : An Agentic Framework for Time Series Forecasting
papers.abstract: Abstract Nexus is a multi-agent forecasting framework that decomposes time series prediction into specialized stages, enabling effective integration of numerical patterns and contextual information for improved forecasting accuracy and explainability.  AI-generated summary Time series forecasting is not just numerical extrapolation, but often requires reasoning with unstructured contextual data such as news or events. While specialized Time Series Foundation Models (TSFMs) excel at forecasting based on numerical patterns, they remain unaware to real-world textual signals. Conversely, while LLMs are emerging as zero-shot forecasters, their performance remains uneven across domains and contextual grounding. To bridge this gap, we introduce Nexus, a multi-agent forecasting framework that decomposes prediction into specialized stages: isolating macro-level and micro-level temporal fluctuations, and integrating contextual information when available before synthesizing a final forecast. This decomposition enables Nexus to adapt from seasonal signals to volatile, event-driven information without relying on external statistical anchors or monolithic prompting. We show that current-generation LLMs possess substantially stronger intrinsic forecasting ability than previously recognized, depending critically on how numerical and contextual reasoning are organized. Evaluated on data strictly succeeding LLM knowledge cutoffs spanning Zillow real estate metrics and volatile stock market equities, Nexus consistently matches or outperforms state-of-the-art TSFMs and strong LLM baselines. Beyond numerical accuracy, Nexus produces high-quality reasoning traces that explicitly show the fundamental drivers behind each forecast. Our results establish that real-world forecasting is an agentic reasoning problem extending well beyond only sequence modeling.

ru.title: Агентное рассуждение для понимающего прогнозирования
ru.desc: Nexus — это многоагентная система для прогнозирования временных рядов, которая разделяет задачу прогнозирования на специализированные этапы: выделение макро- и микро-уровневых колебаний и интеграция текстовой контекстной информации. Система объединяет преимущества специализированных моделей временных рядов, которые хорошо работают с числовыми закономерностями, и больших языковых моделей, которые могут рассуждать о событиях в новостях и контексте. Фреймворк показывает, что современные LLM обладают более сильными встроенными способностями к прогнозированию, чем ранее предполагалось, когда правильно организованы числовые и контекстные рассуждения. Nexus не только обеспечивает точные прогнозы, но и выдает понятные объяснения, показывающие фундаментальные факторы, лежащие в основе каждого прогноза.

en.title: Nexus: Bridging Numerical Patterns and Context for Superior Forecasting
en.desc: Nexus is a multi-agent framework designed for time series forecasting that breaks down the prediction process into specialized stages. It effectively combines numerical data with contextual information, such as news events, to enhance both accuracy and explainability of forecasts. The framework leverages the strengths of Time Series Foundation Models (TSFMs) and large language models (LLMs) by isolating different levels of temporal fluctuations and integrating relevant context. By doing so, Nexus not only improves forecasting performance but also provides clear reasoning behind each prediction, demonstrating that real-world forecasting requires more than just numerical analysis.

zh.title: Nexus：多智能体时间序列预测的创新框架
zh.desc: Nexus是一个多智能体预测框架，它将时间序列预测分解为多个专门阶段，从而有效整合数值模式和上下文信息，以提高预测的准确性和可解释性。时间序列预测不仅仅是数值外推，还需要处理诸如新闻或事件等非结构化的上下文数据。Nexus通过隔离宏观和微观时间波动，并在可用时整合上下文信息，来弥补传统时间序列模型和大型语言模型之间的差距。我们的研究表明，Nexus在房地产和股票市场数据上的表现超越了现有的最先进模型，展示了现实世界预测的复杂性和多样性。

papers.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
papers.abstract: Abstract A quantitative framework called PDI-Bench is introduced for evaluating geometric coherence in generated videos through monocular reconstruction and projective-geometry residuals, revealing geometry-specific failure modes in video generators.  AI-generated summary Generative video models are increasingly studied as implicit world models, yet evaluating whether they produce physically plausible 3D structure and motion remains challenging. Most existing video evaluation pipelines rely heavily on human judgment or learned graders, which can be subjective and weakly diagnostic for geometric failures. We introduce PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence in generated videos. Given a generated clip, we obtain object-centric observations via segmentation and point tracking (e.g., SAM 2, MegaSaM, and CoTracker3), lift them to 3D world-space coordinates via monocular reconstruction, and compute a set of projective-geometry residuals capturing three failure dimensions: scale-depth alignment, 3D motion consistency, and 3D structural rigidity. To support systematic evaluation, we build PDI-Dataset, covering diverse scenarios designed to stress these geometric constraints. Across state-of-the-art video generators, PDI reveals consistent geometry-specific failure modes that are not captured by common perceptual metrics, and provides a diagnostic signal for progress toward physically grounded video generation and physical world model. Our code and dataset can be found at https://pdi-bench.github.io/.

ru.title: Диагностика геометрической согласованности в генеративных видеомоделях через проективную геометрию
ru.desc: В работе представлена система PDI-Bench для количественной оценки геометрической согласованности в сгенерированных видео через монокулярную реконструкцию и анализ остатков проективной геометрии. Авторы получают наблюдения объектов через сегментацию и отслеживание точек, поднимают их в трёхмерное мировое пространство и вычисляют остатки, фиксирующие три типа ошибок: несогласованность масштаба-глубины, непостоянство 3D-движения и нарушение жёсткости структуры. Была создана специализированная PDI-Dataset с разнообразными сценариями для стрессового тестирования геометрических ограничений. Система выявляет специфичные для геометрии режимы отказа современных видеогенераторов, которые не обнаруживаются стандартными перцептивными метриками.

en.title: PDI-Bench: A New Standard for Evaluating Video Geometry
en.desc: The paper introduces PDI-Bench, a new framework for assessing the geometric coherence of videos generated by AI. It focuses on evaluating the physical plausibility of 3D structures and motions in these videos, which is often difficult with existing methods. PDI-Bench uses monocular reconstruction and projective-geometry residuals to identify specific failure modes related to scale-depth alignment, 3D motion consistency, and structural rigidity. This framework aims to provide a more objective and systematic way to evaluate video generation models, moving beyond subjective human assessments.

zh.title: PDI-Bench：评估生成视频几何一致性的量化框架
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为PDI-Bench的定量框架，用于评估生成视频的几何一致性。该框架通过单目重建和投影几何残差，揭示了视频生成器在几何方面的特定失败模式。PDI-Bench能够通过分割和点跟踪获取物体中心的观察，并将其提升到三维世界坐标中，从而计算出一系列捕捉失败维度的投影几何残差。通过构建PDI-Dataset，本文支持系统化评估，帮助识别生成视频中的几何缺陷。

papers.title: SPIN: Structural LLM Planning via Iterative Navigation for Industrial Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract SPIN is a planning wrapper that combines validated DAG planning with prefix-based execution control to reduce task execution and improve plan validity in industrial LLM agent systems.  AI-generated summary Industrial LLM agent systems often separate planning from execution, yet LLM planners frequently produce structurally invalid or unnecessarily long workflows, leading to brittle failures and avoidable tool and API cost. We propose SPIN, a planning wrapper that combines validated Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) planning with prefix based execution control. SPIN enforces a strict DAG contract through \_validate\_plan\_text and repair prompting, producing executable plans before downstream execution, and then evaluates DAG prefixes incrementally to stop when the current prefix is sufficient to answer the query. On AssetOpsBench, across 261 scenarios, SPIN reduces executed tasks from 1061 to 623 and improves Accomplished from 0.638 to 0.706, while reducing tool calls from 11.81 to 6.82 per run. On MCP Bench, the same wrapper improves planning, grounding, and dependency related scores for both GPT OSS1 and Llama 4 Maverick.

ru.title: Оптимизация планов LLM агентов через валидацию и префиксное выполнение
ru.desc: SPIN — это оболочка планирования, которая объединяет валидированное планирование на основе ориентированных ациклических графов (DAG) с префиксным управлением выполнением. Система применяет строгую валидацию структуры плана и исправление промптов, гарантируя получение исполняемых планов до начала выполнения. SPIN инкрементально оценивает префиксы DAG и останавливает выполнение, когда текущего префикса достаточно для ответа на запрос. На практике метод значительно снижает количество выполняемых задач и затраты на вызовы инструментов, одновременно улучшая качество планирования в системах с LLM агентами.

en.title: SPIN: Streamlining Planning and Execution in LLM Systems
en.desc: The paper introduces SPIN, a planning wrapper designed to enhance the efficiency of industrial LLM agent systems by integrating validated Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) planning with prefix-based execution control. This approach addresses the common issue of LLM planners generating invalid or overly complex workflows, which can lead to failures and increased costs. SPIN ensures that plans are validated and executable before execution, using techniques like _validate_plan_text and repair prompting. The results show significant reductions in executed tasks and tool calls, while improving overall performance metrics across various benchmarks.

zh.title: SPIN：提升工业LLM代理系统的规划与执行效率
zh.desc: SPIN是一个规划包装器，它结合了经过验证的有向无环图（DAG）规划和基于前缀的执行控制，以减少任务执行并提高工业大语言模型（LLM）代理系统中的计划有效性。传统上，LLM规划器在规划和执行之间存在分离，导致生成的工作流结构无效或过长，从而引发脆弱的失败和不必要的工具及API成本。SPIN通过_validate_plan_text和修复提示来强制执行严格的DAG合同，确保在下游执行之前生成可执行的计划，并逐步评估DAG前缀，以便在当前前缀足以回答查询时停止。实验结果表明，SPIN在多个场景中显著减少了执行任务的数量，并提高了完成率，同时减少了每次运行的工具调用次数。

papers.title: PreScam: A Benchmark for Predicting Scam Progression from Early Conversations
papers.abstract: Abstract PreScam benchmark enables modeling of scam progression through multi-turn conversations by structuring real-world reports according to a scam kill chain and annotating psychological actions and victim responses.  AI-generated summary Conversational scams, such as romance and investment scams, are emerging as a major form of online fraud. Unlike one-shot scam lures such as fake lottery or unpaid toll messages, they unfold through multi-turn conversations in which scammers gradually manipulate victims using evolving psychological techniques. However, existing research mainly focuses on static scam detection or synthetic scams, leaving open whether language models can understand how real-world scams progress over time. We introduce PreScam, a benchmark for modeling scam progression from early conversations. Built from user-submitted scam reports, PreScam filters and structures 177,989 raw reports into 11,573 conversational scam instances spanning 20 scam categories. Each instance is hierarchically structured according to the scam lifecycle defined by the proposed scam kill chain, and further annotated at the turn level with scammer psychological actions and victim responses. We benchmark models on two tasks: real-time termination prediction, which estimates whether a conversation is approaching the termination stage, and scammer action prediction, which forecasts the scammer's subsequent actions. Results show a clear gap between surface-level fluency and progression modeling: supervised encoders substantially outperform zero-shot LLMs on real-time termination prediction, while next-action prediction remains only moderately successful even for strong LLMs. Taken together, these results show that current models can capture some scam-related cues, yet still struggle to track how risk escalates and how manipulation unfolds across turns.

ru.title: Отслеживание развития мошенничества через многооборотные диалоги
ru.desc: Авторы представляют PreScam — бенчмарк для моделирования развития мошенничества в многооборотных диалогах. Датасет содержит 11,573 реальных примеров мошенничества, структурированных по цепочке жизненного цикла аферы и аннотированных психологическими действиями мошенников и ответами жертв. На бенчмарке тестируются две задачи: предсказание приближения завершения разговора и предсказание следующего действия мошенника. Результаты показывают, что контролируемые энкодеры лучше справляются с прогнозированием терминации, чем LLM, однако ни один из протестированных подходов полностью не решает задачу отслеживания эскалации риска и манипуляции в диалоге.

en.title: Understanding Scam Progression Through Conversations
en.desc: The PreScam benchmark focuses on understanding how scams develop over multiple conversations, particularly through psychological manipulation. It organizes real-world scam reports into a structured format based on a scam kill chain, allowing for detailed analysis of scammer actions and victim responses. The benchmark includes 11,573 conversational instances from 20 different scam categories, enabling researchers to evaluate models on predicting when a scam conversation might end and what actions a scammer might take next. Results indicate that while some models can identify scam cues, they still face challenges in accurately tracking the progression of scams over time.

zh.title: 揭示多轮对话中的诈骗进展
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了PreScam基准，它通过多轮对话建模诈骗进展，结构化真实的诈骗报告。与一次性诈骗不同，多轮对话诈骗通过逐步操控受害者，使用不断演变的心理技巧。研究表明，现有模型在实时终止预测和诈骗者行为预测方面存在明显差距，监督编码器在实时终止预测上表现优于零样本大语言模型。尽管当前模型能够捕捉一些与诈骗相关的线索，但在追踪风险升级和操控过程方面仍然面临挑战。

papers.title: Overcoming Dynamics-Blindness: Training-Free Pace-and-Path Correction for VLA Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Vision-Language-Action models suffer from temporal blindness in dynamic environments, but a training-free correction method using quadratic optimization improves performance by addressing pace and path dynamics simultaneously.  AI-generated summary Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models achieve remarkable flexibility and generalization beyond classical control paradigms. However, most prevailing VLAs are trained under a single-frame observation paradigm, which leaves them structurally blind to temporal dynamics. Consequently, these models degrade severely in non-stationary scenarios, even when trained or finetuned on dynamic datasets. Existing approaches either require expensive retraining or suffer from latency bottlenecks and poor temporal consistency across action chunks. We propose Pace-and-Path Correction, a training-free, closed-form inference-time operator that wraps any chunked-action VLA. From a single quadratic cost, joint minimization yields a unified solution that decomposes orthogonally into two distinct channels. The pace channel compresses execution along the planned direction, while the path channel applies an orthogonal spatial offset, jointly absorbing the perceived dynamics within the chunk window. We evaluate our approach on a comprehensive diagnostic benchmark MoveBench designed to isolate motion as the sole controlled variable. Empirical results demonstrate that our framework consistently outperforms state-of-the-art training-free wrappers and dynamic-adaptive methods and improves success rates by up to 28.8% and 25.9% in absolute terms over foundational VLA models in dynamic-only and static-dynamic mixed environments, respectively.

ru.title: Коррекция темпа и траектории для динамических Vision-Language-Action моделей
ru.desc: В статье рассматривается проблема Vision-Language-Action моделей, которые не способны обрабатывать временную динамику в динамических окружениях. Авторы предлагают метод Pace-and-Path Correction, который работает без переобучения и применяется непосредственно при инференсе через квадратичную оптимизацию. Метод разложенно обрабатывает две компоненты: темп выполнения действий вдоль запланированной траектории и ортогональное пространственное смещение для адаптации к воспринимаемой динамике. Экспериментальные результаты показывают улучшение точности на 28.8% в динамических сценариях и 25.9% в смешанных окружениях по сравнению с базовыми VLA моделями.

en.title: Enhancing VLA Models with Training-Free Temporal Correction
en.desc: This paper addresses the limitations of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models in dynamic environments, where they often struggle due to their reliance on single-frame observations. The authors introduce a novel method called Pace-and-Path Correction, which uses quadratic optimization to enhance the models' performance without the need for retraining. This method effectively separates the dynamics of pace and path, allowing the model to adapt to changes in motion and improve temporal consistency. Experimental results show significant improvements in success rates, demonstrating the effectiveness of this training-free approach in both dynamic and mixed environments.

zh.title: 步伐与路径修正：提升动态环境中的模型性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为“步伐与路径修正”的方法，旨在解决视觉-语言-动作模型在动态环境中的时间盲点问题。该方法通过二次优化实现了训练无关的修正，能够同时处理速度和路径的动态变化。实验结果表明，该方法在动态环境和静态-动态混合环境中，成功率分别提高了28.8%和25.9%。这表明，步伐与路径修正能够显著提升视觉-语言-动作模型的性能，尤其是在非静态场景中。

papers.title: CurveBench: A Benchmark for Exact Topological Reasoning over Nested Jordan Curves
papers.abstract: Abstract CurveBench presents a benchmark for hierarchical topological reasoning using visual inputs, demonstrating significant challenges in exact topology-aware visual reasoning even with advanced models.  AI-generated summary We introduce CurveBench, a benchmark for hierarchical topological reasoning from visual input. CurveBench consists of 756 images of pairwise non-intersecting Jordan curves across easy, polygonal, topographic-inspired, maze-like, and dense counting configurations. Each image is annotated with a rooted tree encoding the containment relations between planar regions. We formulate the task as structured prediction: given an image, a model must recover the full rooted containment tree induced by the curves. Despite the visual simplicity of the task, the strongest evaluated model, Gemini 3.1 Pro, achieves only 71.1\% tree-generation accuracy on CurveBench-Easy and 19.1\% on CurveBench-Hard. We further demonstrate benchmark utility through RLVR-style fine-tuning of open-weight vision-language models. Our trained Qwen3-VL-8B model improves over Qwen-3-VL-8B-Thinking from 2.8\% to 33.3\% tree-generation accuracy on CurveBench-Easy, exceeding GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.5 under our evaluation protocol. The remaining gap, especially on CurveBench-Hard, shows that exact topology-aware visual reasoning remains far from solved.

ru.title: Точное топологическое понимание изображений — задача для моделей будущего
ru.desc: В статье представлен CurveBench — бенчмарк для задачи структурированного предсказания, где модели должны восстанавливать иерархические топологические отношения между областями плоскости на основе изображений кривых Жордана. Несмотря на визуальную простоту задачи, даже передовые модели вроде Gemini 3.1 Pro показывают низкую точность — 71.1% на лёгких примерах и 19.1% на сложных. Авторы демонстрируют практическую полезность бенчмарка через файн-тюнинг открытых vision-language моделей, где Qwen3-VL-8B достигает 33.3% точности после обучения с подкреплением. Исследование подчёркивает, что топологическое визуальное рассуждение остаётся нерешённой проблемой даже для современных больших мультимодальных моделей.

en.title: CurveBench: Tackling Topological Reasoning in Visual Data
en.desc: CurveBench is a new benchmark designed to evaluate hierarchical topological reasoning using visual inputs. It includes 756 images of non-intersecting Jordan curves, each annotated with a rooted tree that represents the containment relationships between different regions. The task is framed as structured prediction, where models must accurately reconstruct the containment tree from the images. Despite advancements in AI, even the best-performing model, Gemini 3.1 Pro, shows limited accuracy, highlighting the ongoing challenges in achieving precise topology-aware visual reasoning.

zh.title: 拓扑推理的新挑战：CurveBench基准
zh.desc: CurveBench是一个用于层次拓扑推理的基准测试，专注于从视觉输入中提取信息。该基准包含756张图像，展示了成对不相交的Jordan曲线，涵盖了多种配置。尽管任务在视觉上看似简单，但最强的模型在生成树的准确率上仍然不高，显示出拓扑感知视觉推理的挑战性。通过对开放权重的视觉-语言模型进行微调，研究者们提高了模型的性能，但仍然存在显著的准确率差距，尤其是在更复杂的任务上。
